repositories.brief_summary: New `Atomic.Array` module, `List.append_map`/`rev_append_map`, stdlib additions, type soundness fixes, and GC improvements merged.
repositories.new_features:
- New **`Atomic.Array` module** added to stdlib, providing atomic arrays with `make`, `init`, `length`, `get`/`set`, `exchange`, `compare_and_set`, and `fetch_and_add` (safe and unsafe variants); joint work by [@clef-men](https://github.com/clef-men) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) ([#14380](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14380))
- New **`List.append_map`** and **`List.rev_append_map`** functions added to stdlib, combining map and append in a single tail-recursive-modulo-cons pass with half the allocations of the composed version; by [Sylvain Boilard](https://github.com/SylvainBoilard) and Hugo Heuzard ([#13554](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13554))
- New **`Set.S.singleton_to_elt`** and **`Map.S.singleton_to_binding`** functions added to the Set and Map modules; by Vincent Laviron ([#14121](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14121))
- **`Hashtbl.is_randomized`** result is now reflected in the `R` parameter of `Sys.runtime_parameters`, making the randomization state observable via the standard runtime parameters string; by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm), [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27), and [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) ([#14292](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14292))
- **`Gc.stat`** is now deprecated; users should call `Gc.quick_stat` directly and explicitly call `Gc.full_major ()` beforehand if up-to-date counters are needed; by [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) ([#12023](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12023))
- **Optimization hints from Lambda** are now kept in bytecode modules and executables (`.cmo`/`.cma`/`.cme`), making them available to Js_of_ocaml and Wasm_of_ocaml; by Jérôme Vouillon ([#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13438))
- **Warning 16** (unerasable-optional-argument) check improved to correctly handle module-dependent functions in the presence of GADTs and first-class modules; by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) ([#14512](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14512), [#14626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14626))
- **Prefetch read/write distinction** in the runtime now correctly distinguishes between prefetching for read vs. write access, with performance benefits for some multi-domain programs; pass `-mprfchw` or `-march=native` to the C compiler for maximum benefit on AMD64; by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) and [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) ([#14570](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14570), [#14616](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14616))
- **Type soundness fix** for `check_counter_example_pat` against polymorphic types that could previously cause segfaults with patterns like `let should_not_work ([ bad ] : 'a. 'a list) = ...`; by Stephen Dolan and [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) ([#14434](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14434), [#14652](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14652))
- **Bytecode effects fix** for a bug causing heap corruption in `reperform` when using `try Effect.perform with Effect.Unhandled` under deep effect handlers; by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) ([#14644](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14644), [#14647](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14647))
- **`Float.Array.sub`/`append`/`concat`** bug fixed where these functions could return a value not equal to `[||]` for empty arrays; by Marc Lasson ([#14635](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14635))
- **UTF-8 normalization** and un/capitalization functions optimized, both within and outside ASCII range; by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) ([#14618](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14618), [#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14620))
- **Continuation objects** now use one field instead of two, reducing allocation overhead for effect handlers; by [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk) ([#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14469))
- **S390x TSan** (Thread Sanitizer) support removed, simplifying the s390x backend; by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) ([#14651](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14651))
- **Lexer directive rule** exhaustiveness fix, resolving an ocamllex warning about the non-exhaustive `directive` rule; by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) ([#14653](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14653), [#14654](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14654))
- **`Random.get_state` documentation** clarified to warn about the pitfall of generating duplicate random numbers when the returned state is passed to multiple generators; by [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) ([#13533](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13533))
- **`HashedType.hash`** documentation clarified to explain the required properties of the hash function, following a report about unexpected behavior when hash functions read the hashtable itself during resize; by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) ([#14660](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14660), [#14663](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14663))
- **Windows documentation** updated to note that the OCaml runtime expands wildcards in arguments before `Sys.argv` is populated, which can surprise users; by [Benjamin Sigonneau](https://github.com/benji-sb) ([#13590](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13590))
repositories.activity_summary: New PRs opened for semantic type error highlighting, ephemeron GC improvements, and prefetch enhancements; ongoing work on minor GC, CFI support, and typing.
repositories.activity:
- Newly opened: [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened a **semantic highlighting PR for type error messages** ([#14659](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14659)) that highlights incompatible subexpressions in type comparisons using color markers, and a related **typechecker error trace refactoring** ([#14536](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14536))
- Newly opened: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) opened **ephemeron quadratic GC blowup fix** ([#14657](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14657)) that eagerly marks data of preserved ephemerons so dependent ephemerons can be detected in the same round, reducing collection time from ~0.5s per cycle to effectively instantaneous on deep dependency chains
- Newly opened: [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) opened **`-Wwrite-strings` C compiler flag** enablement PR ([#14666](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14666)) and **always emit `prefetchw` instruction on x86/x86_64** PR ([#14665](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14665))
- **Faster minor GC** rewrite of `oldify_one` hot path by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) remains under review, promising 2–3% total runtime improvements in promotion-heavy workloads ([#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571))
- **CFI/DWARF debug info** additions for Power ([#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482)) and s390x ([#14500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14500)) architectures by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) continue progressing after s390x TSan removal
- **Native `Iatomic_fetch_add`** code generation extended to all remaining backends (amd64, arm64, riscv, power) by [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) ([#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575)), with benchmarks showing contended multi-domain performance improvements
- [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) continues **build system improvements**: `LN_S`/`AR`/`ARFLAGS` standardization PR ([#14552](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14552)) awaits review
- **Hashtbl default randomization** discussion on PR ([#14291](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14291)) remains active with consensus forming around adding `Hashtbl.create_seeded` and `Hashtbl.create_deterministic` rather than changing the default
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) has a PR open for **`{Sys,Unix}.rename` on Windows** to allow renaming onto read-only files for better POSIX compatibility ([#14602](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14602))
- [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) continues a **typing refactoring series** splitting `type_let` into separate `type_let_nonrec` and `type_let_rec` functions ([#14394](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14394), [#14537](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14537), [#14538](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14538)) as prerequisite work for fixing polymorphic recursion issues
- [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) has PR open to **run expect tests with `-rectypes`** flag ([#14643](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14643)) to catch behavioral differences, showing ~10% testsuite time increase
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) also has a PR for **modular explicit optional argument elision** in the Lambda IR ([#14648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14648))
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) opened a **correct compiler flag ordering fix** for ocamltest ([#14662](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14662))
- **4.14 maintenance fix** for pthread libraries linked correctly in `dllthreads.so` merged by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) ([#14661](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14661))
- Old stale issues closed during triage: [#12425](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12425) (flaky memory-model test), [#12367](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12367) (@@untagged extension covered by another PR), [#12218](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12218) (print/dump value proposal), [#11948](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11948) (ocamllex assembler errors on arm32/ppc64, arm32 retirement from opam-repo-ci now tracked separately)
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussions on Hashtbl randomization defaults, optimal minor heap size, polymorphic type soundness, and a performance regression in Coccinelle.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- The long-running **Hashtbl default randomization** discussion ([#14291](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14291)) saw renewed activity with multiple stakeholders; [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) proposed an alternative approach of tracking insertion order to make iteration deterministic regardless of randomization, while [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) and others expressed concern about breakage comparable to `-safe-string`; consensus appears to be forming around introducing `Hashtbl.create_seeded` and `Hashtbl.create_deterministic` rather than changing the default behavior
- **Minor heap size defaults** discussion ([#14634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14634)) started by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) presents benchmark data showing a current default of 256Kwords (2MiB) is too conservative for modern hardware — `OCAMLRUNPARAM=s=32M` showing up to 3x speedup on allocation-heavy workloads — prompting discussion on what a good new default would be
- **Polymorphic parameters soundness bug** ([#14434](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14434)) discussion concluded after the minimal fix by Stephen Dolan and [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) was chosen over a larger refactoring proposed by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob), with [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) noting the two-timescale consideration (bug-fix speed vs. long-term maintainability)
- **Mutually-recursive function performance** regression in Coccinelle reported by [Victor Gambier](https://github.com/vgambier) ([#14656](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14656)) revealed an interesting threshold effect: chains of 62+ recursive functions cross the 256-word minor-heap allocation boundary, causing the closure to be allocated directly on the major heap with significantly worse GC behavior; [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) identified the root cause
- **Undesirable exponential typechecking time** for programs with deeply-nested tuple types reported by [Arthur Charguéraud](https://github.com/charguer) ([#14650](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14650)); [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) and [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) discussed whether the behavior is inherent to the interaction between deep copying and ambivalence/principality tracking in the type system
- **`Bigarray.reshape` overflow** vulnerability reported by [Andriy Sultanov](https://github.com/last-genius) ([#14655](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14655)) that allows reading uninitialized memory on OCaml 4.14.x; confirmed fixed in OCaml 5 as a side effect of [#11022](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11022); [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) noted this deserves a security advisory for 4.14
repositories.trends_summary: Parallel and concurrent primitives maturing with Atomic.Array; sustained typing improvements; GC optimization work across multiple fronts.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Atomic/parallel primitives** continue expanding — `Atomic.Array` merged ([#14380](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14380)), native `Iatomic_fetch_add` across all backends progressing ([#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575)), and continuation object size reduction ([#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14469)) all indicate sustained investment in multicore performance primitives
- **GC optimization** is a recurring theme this week with ephemeron quadratic blowup fix ([#14657](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14657)), minor GC rewrite ([#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571)), ephemeron budget fix ([#14610](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14610)), and minor heap size discussion ([#14634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14634)) all active simultaneously
- **Type error message quality** is an active area with [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)'s semantic highlighting PR ([#14659](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14659)) built on a typechecker error trace refactoring ([#14536](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14536)), continuing a multi-release effort to make type errors more actionable
- **Cross-platform and build system** work by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) spans multiple PRs (MSVC prefetch docs, lexer fix, `-Wwrite-strings`, `prefetchw` emission, `LN_S`/`AR` standardization), reflecting sustained effort to improve Windows/MSVC and cross-compilation support
- **Typing refactoring towards `let rec` improvements** — [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob)'s multi-PR series ([#14394](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14394), [#14537](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14537), [#14538](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14538)) separating recursive and non-recursive `let` typing is groundwork for fixing long-standing issues with polymorphic recursion ([#14253](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14253))
repositories.issues_summary: One good first issue available: adding source locations to module structures and signatures to improve error messages.
repositories.good_first_issues: - **Store source locations in structures and signatures** ([#12629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12629)): This newcomer-friendly issue asks for adding source location information to module structures and signatures so that type error messages can reference the original `.mli` source file rather than compiled `.cmi` artifacts. Involves small changes to the parser and type-checker touching many files, providing a good tour of a thin slice of the compiler passes. Marked `newcomer-job` and `good first issue` by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).

repositories.brief_summary: Four-PR series by Brian Ward to overhaul opam shell completions using cmdliner 2.1.0 with new PowerShell support.
repositories.activity_summary: Completion system overhaul with cmdliner 2.1.0 upgrade and new PowerShell support, plus Windows and Darcs portability improvements.
repositories.activity:
- [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) opened a four-PR series to modernize opam's shell completion system: [#6854](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6854) adds source compatibility with newer cmdliner versions, [#6867](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6867) updates the vendored cmdliner to 2.1.0 (closing long-standing issue [#684](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/684)), [#6868](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6868) updates argument definitions with completion functions for all opam custom types (including switch-dependent package completions), and [#6869](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6869) installs the new cmdliner-based completions replacing existing bash/zsh scripts and adding new **PowerShell** completion support (closes [#6853](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6853))
- [Virgile Robles](https://github.com/Firobe) opened [#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862) to set `_WIN32_WINNT` to Windows 8.1 in `windows.h` usage, fixing a build failure in MSYS2 environments caused by `GetCurrentProcessToken` availability (reported in tarides/opam-monorepo#421)
- [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal) opened [#6866](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6866) to remove the decade-old `Num` patches fallback in `OpamDarcs`, eliminating dead code from 2016 (darcs 2.10.3)
- The long-running WIP performance PR [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) — which restructures HTTP repository handling to use `tar.gz` files directly via `ocaml-tar` rather than untarring — received a fix from [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) for an infinite loop in the tar stream handling
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussions covered solver API usage, an opam publish plugin edge case, and new feature wishes for opam admin and opam install.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- In [#6454](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6454), [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) asked how to set solver criteria via the OCaml API when using `OpamSwitchCommand.create`; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) clarified that `OpamSolverConfig.update ~solver_preferences_default:"..." ()` is the correct approach (not `~criteria`) for opam-solver 2.5.0
- [John Whitington](https://github.com/johnwhitington) reported [#6870](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6870) where `opam publish` used an erroneous archive URL (`libc.tar.gz`) when run automatically after plugin installation, though the issue could not be reproduced on a clean machine
- [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) opened [#6865](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6865) requesting `opam admin add-conflict` to complement the existing `add-constraint` command; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) confirmed it would be straightforward to implement and invited contributions
repositories.trends_summary: Development is focused on cross-platform improvements (Windows/PowerShell) and modernizing the completion and repository infrastructure.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- A sustained push toward **cross-platform completeness**: the cmdliner completion series adds PowerShell support ([#6869](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6869)) and the Windows `windows.h` fix ([#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862)) addresses MSYS2 build failures, both targeting Windows users ahead of the 2.6.0~alpha1 release
- **Modernizing vendored dependencies**: upgrading cmdliner from 1.3.0 to 2.1.0 ([#6867](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6867)) reflects broader effort to keep opam's vendored libraries current
- **Removing legacy code**: both the Darcs `Num` patches fallback ([#6866](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6866)) and aspects of the repository tarring PR ([#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)) continue a pattern of eliminating decade-old workarounds
repositories.issues_summary: Two issues tagged as good first issues: implementing opam admin add-conflict and enabling opam install from a standalone opam file.
repositories.good_first_issues:
- [#6865](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6865) — add `opam admin add-conflict` subcommand; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) described it as mostly a copy-paste of `add-constraint` changing `depends` to `conflicts`, and [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) expressed intent to attempt it
- [#4227](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/4227) — support `opam install <path-to-opam-file>` for standalone opam files that contain a `url` field, useful for testing packages before submitting to opam-repository; workarounds exist but the feature would improve the workflow significantly
repositories.contributors_summary: Brian Ward drove completion system work; Kate, Virgile Robles, toastal, Raphaël Proust, and John Whitington each contributed PRs or issues.

repositories.brief_summary: Release of 1.26.0-5.5~preview with OCaml 5.5 compatibility work, destruct custom request, and merlin build system configuration.
repositories.new_features_summary: Preview release adds destruct custom request, merlin build-system config via env vars, and OCaml 5.5 parsetree adaptations.
repositories.new_features:
- The `1.26.0-5.5~preview` release introduces a new **`destruct` custom request** ([#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583)), exposing Merlin's destruct functionality through a dedicated LSP custom method.
- **Merlin build system configuration** is now supported via environment variables: setting `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM` causes ocaml-lsp to execute `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM ocaml-merlin` to obtain merlin configuration, and `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_ROOT` allows overriding the project root directory. This enables use with build systems other than Dune.
- **Improved `signatureHelp` readability** for long signatures ([#1580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1580)) included in the preview release.
- **OCaml 5.5 parsetree adaptations** in the `550-preview` branch ([#1597](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1597)): `folding_range.ml` and `semantic_highlighting.ml` updated to handle the new `Pexp_struct_item` constructor replacing `Pexp_letmodule`; `action_extract.ml` updated to remove `Texp_letmodule`, `Texp_letexception`, and `Texp_open` from enclosing binder patterns.
- **Semantic highlighting** gains `Ptyp_functor` support and handles the new `Ptype_external` type kind introduced in OCaml 5.5 ([9b331593](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/commit/9b331593deab65f1e24ed657abbd9955980a0e9d)).
- **`dune-rpc` API migration**: `dune.ml` updated to use the new `Drpc.Request.Id.make` and `Drpc.Request.Initialize.create` APIs, with the `Chan` module refactored to separate `close` and `stop` operations ([e5d0d90a](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/commit/e5d0d90a7e4c7cacc9aac029b6c58f457791604d)).
repositories.activity_summary: OCaml 5.5 preview release published with three open PRs for Dune 3.22 compatibility, parsetree changes, and flake updates.
repositories.activity:
- **OCaml 5.5 preview** PR [#1597](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1597) opened by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) adapts ocaml-lsp to OCaml 5.5 parsetree changes; currently has merge conflicts and is under review by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos), who noted the need to handle new `Texp_struct_item` cases and the lost `let module ... in` case.
- **Dune 3.22 compatibility** PR [#1594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1594) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) fixes compilation with Dune 3.22; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) noted that dependency constraints also need updating before merge.
- **Automated flake lock update** PR [#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437) by `github-actions` updates nixpkgs and merlin5_3 inputs to March 2026 revisions.
- **OCaml 5.5 support tracking issue** [#1596](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1596) opened by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) noting that ocaml-lsp-server does not yet support OCaml 5.5 (currently in alpha1).
- Issue [#1586](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1586) ("Unable to find declaration of type when editor position is in another declaration") closed by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) in favor of its Merlin counterpart at [ocaml/merlin#2033](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2033).
- Ongoing discussion on [#1209](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1209) (spurious "Unbound module" errors after editing) where [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) investigated whether `dune-rpc` reports `Progress.Success` before build artifacts are readable, with input from [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussion on spurious 'Unbound module' diagnostic issue and review feedback on the OCaml 5.5 preview PR.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) and [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) investigated the root cause of spurious `Unbound module` diagnostics in [#1209](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1209), specifically whether `Progress.Success` from dune-rpc is emitted before build artifacts are fully readable. Rudi noted existing RPC bugs around diagnostics but considered the timing explanation unlikely.
- Review of [#1597](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1597) by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) flagged missing handling of new `Texp_struct_item` cases and the lost `let module ... in` construct, with notes that some omissions are acceptable for a preview release given the downstream removal of the related code action ([#1546](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1546)).
repositories.trends_summary: Development focus is on OCaml 5.5 compatibility, with active adaptation to parsetree changes across multiple files.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **OCaml 5.5 migration** is the dominant theme: the `Pexp_letmodule` → `Pexp_struct_item` parsetree change requires updates across `folding_range.ml`, `semantic_highlighting.ml`, and `action_extract.ml`, signaling broader compatibility work ahead for the stable 5.5 release.
- The introduction of a **preview release track** (`1.26.0-5.5~preview`) alongside the stable branch reflects a strategy of early compatibility validation before OCaml 5.5 reaches a stable release.
- **Dependency constraint tightening** (pinning `merlin-lib` to `5.7-505~preview` and bumping `dune-rpc` to `>= 3.22.0`) indicates increasing coupling to upstream pre-release packages in the preview branch.
repositories.contributors_summary: Ulysse drove the preview release and reviews; Xavier Van de Woestyne opened the 5.5 parsetree PR; Antonio Nuno Monteiro submitted the Dune 3.22 fix.
repositories.contributors:
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) published the `1.26.0-5.5~preview` release, authored the 550-preview branch commits adapting the codebase to OCaml 5.5 (parsetree changes, CI updates, dune-rpc API migration), reviewed [#1597](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1597), closed [#1586](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1586) redirecting it to Merlin, and participated in debugging [#1209](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1209).
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) opened [#1597](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1597) with OCaml 5.5 parsetree adaptations, and responded to review feedback from [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) with references to related PRs [#1594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1594) and [#1546](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1546).
- [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) submitted [#1594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1594) to fix compilation with Dune 3.22.
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened issue [#1596](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1596) tracking OCaml 5.5 support.
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) provided review feedback on [#1594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1594) and contributed to the [#1209](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1209) diagnostic debugging discussion.

repositories.brief_summary: Portability fix for GNU Hurd builds, test coverage additions, and ocamlformat upgrade in progress.
repositories.new_features_summary: PATH_MAX portability fix for GNU Hurd and new type-enclosing regression test added.
repositories.new_features:
- **`PATH_MAX` portability fix** in `ocamlmerlin.c` defines the constant to 8192 when undefined (e.g., on GNU Hurd), preventing build failures on systems that don't define `PATH_MAX` ([#2039](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2039))
- **New regression test** for `type-enclosing` command documents the inconsistent type variable behavior from [#1949](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1949), where hovering `foo a b = b` shows `'a -> 'b -> 'b` but hovering `b` incorrectly returns `'a` ([#2004](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2004))
repositories.activity_summary: ocamlformat 0.29.0 upgrade PR opened; two PRs merged for portability and test coverage; dependabot PRs closed.
repositories.activity:
- **ocamlformat 0.29.0 upgrade** opened by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) ([#2047](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2047)), currently under review with discussion about the extent of formatting changes and opam availability of the new version
- **GNU Hurd portability fix** merged: [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu)'s `PATH_MAX` definition patch ([#2039](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2039)) was rebased and merged by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) with an added changelog entry
- **Type-enclosing test case** merged from [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) ([#2004](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2004)), capturing the inconsistent type variable behavior documented in [#1949](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1949)
- **Three dependabot PRs closed** without merge: bumps for `cachix/install-nix-action` ([#2046](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2046)), `actions/download-artifact` ([#2040](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2040)), and `actions/upload-artifact` ([#2041](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2041))
- **Issue #2042** (`Envaux.env_from_summary` crash fetching docs for `bos`/`containers`) closed after the reporter confirmed the error disappeared after upgrading their custom `ocaml-lsp-server` build
- **Issue #2043** (unexpected cmi loading check) closed, with test added in commit `2f01715`
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on PR #2047 about ocamlformat 0.29.0 availability and scope of reformatting changes.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) opened [#2047](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2047) to upgrade ocamlformat to 0.29.0; [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) raised questions about the volume of reformatting changes and about `opam install ocamlformat.0.29.0` succeeding in CI before the package appeared available locally — ultimately attributed to a caching delay
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) provided a detailed code-level analysis in [#2033](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2033) explaining why `locate` incorrectly returns "already at origin" when the cursor is on a different type declaration, pointing to the relevant heuristic in `src/analysis/locate.ml#L835-L859`
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on test coverage for existing known bugs and portability improvements for non-mainstream platforms.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Test-first documentation of known bugs**: multiple PRs this week ([#2004](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2004), and ongoing [#2034](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2034), [#2002](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2002)) add regression tests that capture current incorrect behavior before fixes land, making future improvements verifiable
- **Portability broadening**: the `PATH_MAX` fix ([#2039](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2039)) reflects ongoing attention to building Merlin on non-Linux systems such as GNU Hurd
repositories.issues_summary: One good first issue open: locate command returns wrong result when cursor is in a different type declaration.
repositories.good_first_issues: - [#2033](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2033) **"Unable to find declaration of type when editor position is in another declaration"** (opened by [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d), related to [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1586](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1586)): the `locate` command incorrectly short-circuits when the cursor sits inside a type declaration, returning "already at origin" even for a different type. [@voodoos](https://github.com/voodoos) has left a detailed comment explaining the relevant code path in `src/analysis/locate.ml#L835-L859` and suggesting a fix approach, making this a well-scoped entry point for new contributors
repositories.contributors_summary: Three contributors active: Stéphane Glondu with a portability patch, Tim ats with test additions, and voodoos with reviews and merges.
repositories.contributors:
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) contributed the `PATH_MAX` portability fix for GNU Hurd ([#2039](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2039))
- [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) added the `type-enclosing` regression test ([#2004](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2004)) and has open PRs adding further reproduction cases for [#2033](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2033) ([#2034](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2034)) and [#1786](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1786) ([#2002](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2002))
- [@voodoos](https://github.com/voodoos) merged both incoming PRs ([#2039](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2039), [#2004](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2004)), added the changelog entry for the Hurd fix, opened the ocamlformat upgrade PR ([#2047](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2047)), and provided code-level review guidance on [#2033](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2033) and [#2002](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2002)
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) reviewed [#2047](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2047), asking clarifying questions about the reformatting scope and the opam package availability

repositories.brief_summary: Dune 3.22.0 was released on March 18, followed by an extensive internal cache and module restructuring effort.
repositories.new_features_summary: New features include a @revdep alias for building reverse dependencies and the ability to promote file prefixes with dune promote.
repositories.new_features:
- [#13800](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13800) merged a `@revdep` alias (also `@revdep-check`, `@revdep-runtest`) that builds all reverse dependencies of libraries in a directory, useful when refactoring.
- [#13876](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13876) proposes allowing `dune promote foo` to promote a path prefix and all descendants under `foo/`, with a `--file` flag to restore the old single-file behaviour.
- [#13885](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13885) opens work on explicit patch-back for sandboxed rules in dune 3.23, following a test PR [#13882](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13882) that documents its current broken state.
- [#13813](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13813) proposes a per-rule opt-in that lets sandboxed actions produce `.corrected` files for promotions without needing to declare targets.
- [#13880](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13880) proposes extending the `diff` action to support diffing directories, enabling generic test rules over a directory of test cases.
repositories.activity_summary: The week saw the 3.22.0 release land, followed by a sustained cache/module refactoring push and several Windows package-management fixes.
repositories.activity:
- [#13844](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13844) (open) adds CI jobs to check package dependency specifications and lower bounds for each `.opam` package, addressing [#13731](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13731) and [#13821](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13821).
- [#13876](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13876) (open) allows promoting path prefixes with `dune promote`.
- [#13885](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13885) (open) begins explicit patch-back support for dune 3.23.
- [#13875](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13875) (open) moves per-target digest storage into the rule cache, removing reliance on the stat cache for build targets.
- [#13880](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13880) (open) proposes directory diffing in the `diff` action.
- [#13842](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13842) (open) fixes missing dependencies in compilation rules when sandboxing with a `root_module`.
- [#13833](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13833) (open) fixes a spurious dependency cycle with `enabled_if` and `%{read:...}` when a `package` stanza is present; authored by [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots).
- [#13864](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13864) (open) fixes `.exe` handling in `.install` files on Windows, fixing [#12430](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12430).
- [#13873](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13873) (open) simplifies the changelog update process for releases.
- [#13769](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13769) merged, preparing and releasing dune 3.22.0.
- [#13884](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13884) merged, fixing incorrect value sharing when serialising the targets digest database.
- [#13883](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13883) merged, wrapping `Stdlib.Marshal` in a safer interface with an explicit `~sharing` parameter.
- [#13854](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13854) merged, merging `dune_rpc_private` into `dune_rpc` and promoting internal `.mli` files to the library's public surface.
- [#13840](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13840) merged, merging `dune_cache_storage` into `dune_cache` and removing the now-redundant split.
- [#13856](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13856) merged, fixing Windows binary lookup by stripping `.exe` from map keys when populating the binary cookie, fixing [#12433](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12433).
- [#13800](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13800) merged, adding the `@revdep` alias family.
- [#13838](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13838) merged, using `open_out_bin` in the configure step to avoid CRLF line endings on Windows.
- [#13785](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13785) merged, substituting the version variable in package string interpolation when a package is absent from the solution.
- [#13828](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13828) merged, removing Coq test files ahead of the planned removal of Coq support in 3.24.
repositories.discussions_summary: Ongoing discussions focus on Windows compatibility gaps, a spurious cycle with enabled_if, and the future of package management CI.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#13871](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13871) reports that dune 3.22.0 fails to bootstrap on Windows 7 because `GetSystemTimePreciseAsFileTime` (introduced in Windows 8) is now referenced; [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) is preparing a fallback.
- [#13832](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13832) reports a dependency cycle when a library uses `enabled_if` with `%{read:...}` and a `package` directive; [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) opened a fix in [#13833](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13833).
- [#13674](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13674) (the 3.22.0 release tracker) was closed after the release; discussion surfaced that `stdune` breaking changes affect `ocaml-lsp-server` and that `stdune` may eventually be made private.
- [#13844](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13844) discussions debate whether the new CI package-check jobs should run on every PR or only on merges to main, given 20-30 minute job times.
- [#13823](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13823) and [#13825](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13825) track OpenBSD `tar` misdetection: dune incorrectly detects archive types from a temp-file path instead of the original filename, causing `-z` to be omitted.
repositories.trends_summary: An accelerating internal refactoring is consolidating cache, fiber, and RPC modules while Windows package management support continues to be hardened.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- A sustained cache-consolidation effort merged `dune_cache_storage` into `dune_cache`, eliminated value-storage code, hid `Fs_cache` behind `Fs_memo`, and wrapped `Stdlib.Marshal` in a safer API, collectively reducing overall module count and removing dead code.
- Several Windows-specific package management fixes landed (`.exe` stripping, CRLF avoidance, binary-lookup correction), reflecting increased investment in Windows support post-3.22.0.
- Work is underway on better sandbox interaction: patch-back, corrections from sandboxed actions, and directory diff all target improvements to how sandboxed rules interact with promotion.
- CI infrastructure is being strengthened with per-package dependency and lower-bounds checking jobs, motivated by two consecutive release regressions.
- The draft odoc v3 rules PR [#12995](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/12995) continues to receive review, aiming to unify `@doc` and `@doc-new` with sidebar, hierarchical docs, and sherlodoc integration.
repositories.issues_summary: Several package-management bugs were reported including lock-dir path handling, absolute paths in pinned local dependencies, and archive-type misdetection.
repositories.good_first_issues:
- [#13815](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13815) asks for an informative error when the `patch` action is used on a package without a `source` stanza in its lock file.
- [#13814](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13814) asks for `dune show workspace_root` and `dune show project_root` subcommands to make scripting easier.
- [#13818](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13818) suggests that `dune pkg lock` should prefer stronger hashes (sha256/sha512) over MD5 when multiple checksums are available in opam metadata; a patch is provided by the reporter.
repositories.contributors_summary: Rudi Grinberg led the week with a large volume of cache-refactoring merges; Ali Caglayan drove Windows fixes and the 3.22.0 release.
repositories.contributors:
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) merged an extensive sequence of cache, fiber, and RPC refactoring PRs as well as the `@revdep` alias feature and several bug fixes.
- [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) handled the 3.22.0 release preparation, Windows binary-lookup and CRLF fixes, and removal of legacy Coq tests.
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) opened the changelog simplification PR, the release tracker, and CI package-check work.
- [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) opened the CI jobs PR for package dependency and lower-bounds checking and added a test reproducing the `lock_dir` path bug.
- [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) opened the `root_module` sandbox fix PR [#13842](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13842).
- [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) reported and fixed the `enabled_if` cycle with package stanzas in [#13833](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13833).
- [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS) opened a PR clarifying `Io.links` documentation [#13791](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13791) and is working on directory-symlink resolution in package fetching [#13792](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13792).
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) reviewed Windows-related changes and is preparing a Windows 7 compatibility fallback.

repositories.brief_summary: Issue filed about duplicate ld.conf warnings appearing with OCaml 5.5 when invoking ocamlfind.
repositories.activity_summary: One issue opened reporting spurious double warnings about unreadable stublibs directories in ld.conf under OCaml 5.5.
repositories.activity: - [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened [#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115) reporting that `ocamlfind ocamlopt` on OCaml 5.5 emits a **duplicate pair of warnings** — `Cannot read directory ../stublibs` and `Cannot read directory ./stublibs` — on every invocation, with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) cc'd as a likely subject-matter expert.
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion centred on spurious ld.conf warnings affecting OCaml 5.5 users invoking ocamlfind directly.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115) highlights that OCaml 5.5 users calling `ocamlfind ocamlopt` by hand now see **two repeated warnings** about unreadable `stublibs` paths listed in `ld.conf`; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) was cc'd for follow-up, suggesting the root cause may lie in how the new OCaml 5.5 installation layout populates or references `ld.conf`.
repositories.trends_summary: Compatibility friction between ocamlfind and OCaml 5.5 installation layout surfacing via user-reported warnings.
repositories.emerging_trends: - The **OCaml 5.5 compatibility** theme continues to surface, with [#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115) indicating that changes to the standard installation directory structure in OCaml 5.5 can cause ocamlfind to emit spurious `ld.conf` path warnings, pointing to a need for ocamlfind to handle missing or relative stublibs entries more gracefully.

repositories.activity:

repositories.brief_summary: OCaml 4.14.3 LTS release page added with CVE-2026-28364 security fix; dune 3.22 announcement and Planet scrapes ongoing.
repositories.new_features_summary: OCaml 4.14.3 release page published as new LTS, fixing CVE-2026-28364 Marshal deserialization vulnerability.
repositories.new_features: - **OCaml 4.14.3 release page** added to the website, marking it as the current Long Term Support release and transferring the `is_lts` designation from 4.14.1 and 4.14.2 ([#3571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3571)). The page highlights a **security fix for [CVE-2026-28364](https://www.cve.org/CVERecord?id=CVE-2026-28364)** (Marshal deserialization buffer over-read, OSEC-2026-01) and documents several bug fixes backported from OCaml 5.x, including: quadratic closure computation made linear ([ocaml/ocaml#12207](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12207)), memory-safety on `Lazy.force` races ([ocaml/ocaml#13430](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13430)), code-generation bug on unsafe array accesses for int32/int64/nativeint ([ocaml/ocaml#13448](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13448)), and memory corruption on Marshal exception ([ocaml/ocaml#14007](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14007)).
repositories.activity_summary: Two PRs opened and awaiting merge: markdown lint fixes for 4.14.x release data and the dune 3.22 release announcement.
repositories.activity:
- **Dune 3.22 release announcement** opened by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) ([#3570](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3570)), adding 248 lines of release notes content — awaiting merge.

repositories.brief_summary: A major week dominated by the dune 3.22.0 release, large scientific computing package raven 1.0.0~alpha3, and a wave of Windows/MSYS2 support improvements.
repositories.new_features_summary: Notable new packages include parseff (algebraic-effect parser combinators for OCaml 5), jws (RFC7515 JWS implementation), and dnsrobot (DNS/network tools client).
repositories.new_features:
- **[parseff 0.1.0 and 0.2.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29526)** merged — direct-style parser combinator library for OCaml 5 powered by algebraic effects, with zero-copy span APIs and domain-safe operation; a follow-up [0.2.0 release](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29573) unified `*1` combinators into base combinators with `~at_least` parameter
- **[jws 0.0.1](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29557)** merged — initial release of an RFC 7515 JWS implementation using jsont and mirage-crypto, later used as a dependency in letsencrypt 2.0.0
- **[dnsrobot 0.1.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29541)** merged — official OCaml client for DNS Robot providing DNS lookups, WHOIS, SSL checks, and 50+ network tools via an Lwt-based async API
- **[dune-ai-context 0.1.2](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29536)** merged — tool that extracts dune external dependencies and generates interface context for AI-assisted coding tools
- **[tm-grammars 1.1.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29548)** merged — massive expansion adding TextMate grammar definitions for 200+ languages as OCaml strings (replacing the broken 1.0.0 tarball)
- **[raven 1.0.0~alpha3](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29490)** merged — major scientific computing release with 11 packages including `nx-oxcaml` (pure-OCaml SIMD tensor backend) and `kaun-board` (TUI training dashboard), plus a unified `Nx.t`/`Rune.t` tensor type
repositories.activity_summary: Dune 3.22.0 released after two alpha/RC iterations, triggering a cascade of upper-bound fixes across ocaml-lsp-server, stdune, ocamlformat, and other dependents.
repositories.activity:
- **Open PRs awaiting review:** [ppxlib and ppxlib-tools 0.38.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29563) — adds OCaml 5.5 support, bivariant type parameters, and labeled tuple utilities; [Ortac 0.8.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29564) — adds QCheck-STM+Domains parallel testing support; [lsp 1.26.0-5.5~preview](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29565) — adds a destruct custom request and support for non-dune build systems via `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM`; [gendarme 0.4.1 (9 packages)](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29566) — marshalling library with ppxlib>=0.36 support; [alcobar 0.3](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29556) — Alcotest-compatible fuzzing library forked from Crowbar; [libinput 1.29](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29574) — OCaml bindings for libinput with broader version support back to libinput 1.20; [oniguruma 0.2.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29583) — breaking update to Oniguruma regex bindings adding new syntaxes and search options; [spectrum 1.0.0.alpha2 (5 packages)](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29581) — terminal color and formatting library suite; [wcwidth 1.1.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29580) — terminal display width calculation
- **[dune 3.22.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29562)** merged — major release with 18 packages, featuring extensive build tracing, expanded sandboxing defaults, oxcaml `.cms` file support, and many bug fixes; preceded by alpha1 and alpha2 candidate PRs that were closed during the stabilization process
- **[atd 4.0.0 (11 packages)](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29552)** merged — **major release** introducing experimental module support with `from ... import ...` syntax, a new `atdml` tool as the recommended successor to `atdgen` for OCaml JSON, and a minimum requirement of Dune >= 3.18
- **[ocamlformat 0.29.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29553)** merged — adds OCaml 5.5 syntax support, updates to Odoc 3.0 parser, adds `letop-punning` option, and sets OCaml version default to 5.4
- **[mirage-crypto 2.1.0 (7 packages)](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29545)** merged — adds `Mirage_crypto_ec.Dsa.Primitive` exposing NIST curve primitives useful for protocols like SPAKE2
- **[letsencrypt 2.0.0 (4 packages)](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29572)** merged — removes yojson in favour of jws/jsont, and decouples the core from lwt/uri via functor-based scheduler abstraction
- **[ocaml-version 4.0.4](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29567)** merged — library for parsing and manipulating OCaml compiler version strings
- **[Gospel 0.3.1](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29559)** merged — OCaml specification language update for ppxlib and cmdliner compatibility
- **[elpi 3.6.2](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29558)** merged — embeddable λProlog interpreter; fixes quadratic growth in program signature storage
- **[charrua 3.1.1 (4 packages)](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29538)** merged — DHCP library bugfix for cancellation semantics in `listen`; [charrua-client 3.0.0 and 3.1.0 marked `avoid-version`](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29570) due to MirageOS network stack runtime issues
- **[mirage-runtime lwt constraint relaxed](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29571)** merged — allows lwt >= 6 when dune >= 3.22 is used, unblocking mirage unikernels from the lwt < 6 restriction
- **[xapi-backtrace 0.8](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29542)** merged — adds license, fixes backtrace loss on reraise, adds regression tests
- **[camlpdf 2.9 and cpdf 2.9](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29546)** merged — PDF library and command-line tools update
- **Dune 3.22 compatibility fixes:** upper bounds added to [stdune](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29561) and [dune-rpc](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29555) for breaking API changes; [ocamlformat constraints on Dune 3.22](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29568) pending due to changed `(diff)` rule behaviour with non-existent files
- **Package metadata:** [various packages metadata update](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29550) including tarball URLs and `x-maintenance-intent` fields; [tm-grammars 1.0.0 marked unavailable](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29554) due to broken release tarball
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussions on MSYS2 Windows support, the lwt 6 / dune 3.22 dependency interaction in MirageOS, and ongoing lwt 6 ecosystem compatibility work.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **[MSYS2 depext gaps](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29575)** — [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) filed four issues ([#29575](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29575), [#29576](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29576), [#29577](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29577), [#29578](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29578)) covering missing MSYS2 depext declarations for conf-m4, conf-perl, conf-findutils, and the need to support UCRT64 in conf-mingw-w64 packages as MSYS2 deprecated MINGW64 in March 2026; a companion PR [#29579](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29579) addresses all four with package-prefix variable interpolation
- **[New ocaml-option-clang-cl package](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29582)** — [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) proposes a marker package enabling MSVC clang-cl as the OCaml compiler's CC, following the pattern of ocaml-option-musl, affecting 16 compiler packages
- **[MirageOS lwt/dune dependency puzzle](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29569)** — [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) asked how to express conditional lwt version constraints depending on dune version; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) and [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) explained the disjunctive constraint pattern `"dune" {>= "3.22"} | "lwt" {< "6"}`, with a note that 0install solver gives slight priority to earlier disjunction terms
- **[Refine constraints on lwt 6](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29530)** — [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) continues broad lwt 6 compatibility work spanning 84 files; discussion covers how to accurately detect which packages need lwt < 6 using opam-health-check-ng
- **[opam switch create with ocaml-option-tsan installs BER MetaOCaml](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/28249)** — ongoing discussion on solver heuristics where MetaOCaml variants are selected unexpectedly; suggestion to add `flags: avoid-version` to MetaOCaml packages
repositories.trends_summary: Windows/MSYS2 support is receiving sustained attention alongside the dune 3.22 release driving a wave of upstream constraint fixes across the ecosystem.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Windows and MSYS2 ecosystem support** is receiving focused attention: UCRT64 environment support for mingw-w64 packages, missing MSYS2 depext declarations for common conf-* packages, and a new `ocaml-option-clang-cl` marker package for MSVC clang-cl — all driven by the MSYS2 project deprecating MINGW64 in March 2026
- **Dune 3.22 ripple effects** — the major dune release required coordinated upper-bound fixes in stdune, dune-rpc, ocaml-lsp-server, and ocamlformat, illustrating how a single build system release can cascade across the package ecosystem
- **OCaml 5 / algebraic effects adoption** — new packages like parseff demonstrate continued growth of the algebraic-effects programming style; raven's nx-oxcaml backend shows pure-OCaml SIMD via OxCaml's unboxed types approaching C backend performance
- **MirageOS dependency modernisation** — letsencrypt 2.0.0 drops yojson, mirage-runtime relaxes the lwt < 6 constraint, and charrua buggy versions are being avoided, reflecting ongoing MirageOS ecosystem cleanup
- **Scientific computing ecosystem growth** — raven 1.0.0~alpha3 introduces a comprehensive scientific computing suite with ML-oriented packages (kaun, brot/tokenization, quill REPL) positioning OCaml for numerical and ML workloads

repositories.brief_summary: Variable coalescing and Wasm bigstring optimizations merged, reducing JS register usage by up to 91% on large programs.
repositories.new_features_summary: New `var-coalescing` compiler pass and flag; Wasm bigstring primitives optimized; assignment operator output added.
repositories.new_features:
- New **variable coalescing optimization pass** (`--enable/--disable var-coalescing`) merges JavaScript variables with disjoint lifespans, reducing V8 register usage by 28–91% when combined with the existing constant sinking pass — reduces the largest function in PRT from 13,539 to 1,263 registers ([#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166))
- New **`var-coalescing` debug section** added to `--debug` options for inspecting compiler internals ([#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166))
- **Wasm bigstring primitives** optimized in `runtime/wasm/bigstring.wat` including a DataView.prototype.getInt32 workaround for non-V8 engines, reducing overhead for bigstring operations ([#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144))
- **Assignment operator output**: compiler now emits `x += y` style assignment operators where possible, producing slightly smaller JavaScript output ([`700661d`](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/700661d26e3ee08300c95c7babd12a992f6de10d))
- New **`Code.Var.forget_generated_name`** API added to the compiler library ([`de6a03a`](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/de6a03ab4754dd8d2953f90650a8ee57e9a8843a))
- New **`pqueue` module** (priority queue) added to the compiler library, used internally by the variable coalescing pass ([#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166))
repositories.activity_summary: Active development on Wasm features (dynlink, native effects, WASI) and compiler improvements (OCaml 5.5, OxCaml, subst speedup).
repositories.activity:
- **Ongoing Wasm dynlink/toplevel unification** work in PR [#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon), with [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) adding commits to align Wasm and JS toplevel behavior; review discussion active this week on exception registration, hash tables, and binary search tradeoffs
- **OCaml 5.5 initial support** in draft PR [#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) with 12,000+ lines of changes across 89 files, currently blocked by merge conflicts
- **OxCaml support** in long-running PR [#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) saw review activity this week from [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) covering CI matrix conditions, ocamlformat compatibility, and attribute-based conditional compilation
- **Wasm native effects** via the Stack Switching proposal in PR [#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon), blocked on [#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177) and [ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613); discussion this week noted that Node 26 removed `--experimental-wasm-stack-switching` (now always enabled) and proposed documenting per-node feature flags
- **WASI runtime** in PR [#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon), supporting `--enable wasi` and `--enable exnref` flags for producing binaries runnable by Wizard and wasmtime engines, currently has merge conflicts
- **Compiler subst speedup** in PR [#2185](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2185) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) and **block sharing preservation** in PR [#2184](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2184) both awaiting merge
- **Variable coalescing assertion fix** for empty try bodies merged ([#2201](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2201)) and **inline duplication fix** for `inline_recursively` path merged ([#2200](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2200)), both addressing edge cases exposed by the new coalescing and arity-tracking work from [#2198](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2198)
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on Node.js flag deprecation for Wasm features and request to document per-version WebAssembly capability flags.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- In PR [#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189), [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) and [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) discussed the removal of `--experimental-wasm-stack-switching` and `--experimental-wasm-imported-strings` flags in Node 26, with [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) noting JSPI is enabled by default since Node 25; [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) proposed maintaining a documentation table of required flags per Node version and browser
- In PR [#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144), [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) referenced a [Mozilla bug](https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1938149) on DataView optimization, noting that benchmarks are pressuring engine implementers to optimize this path and the workaround should eventually be removable
repositories.trends_summary: Sustained focus on JavaScript output quality (coalescing, sinking, assignment operators) and expanding Wasm target capabilities.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Continued investment in **JavaScript output size and efficiency**: variable coalescing ([#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166)) follows the constant sinking pass ([#2167](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2167)) and assignment operator output, forming a series of complementary optimizations targeting V8 register pressure and code size
- Expanding **Wasm target capabilities**: multiple large PRs in flight simultaneously — dynlink/toplevel ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)), native effects via stack switching ([#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189)), and WASI runtime ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)) — indicate a push to bring the Wasm backend to feature parity with the JS backend
- **Upstream OCaml tracking**: parallel work on OCaml 5.5 ([#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195)) and OxCaml ([#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)) indicates effort to keep js_of_ocaml current with upstream compiler developments
repositories.contributors_summary: Jérôme Vouillon merged four PRs this week; @hhugo contributed review feedback and has several open PRs awaiting merge.
