repositories.brief_summary: Heavy week of metaprogramming merges, reaper fixes, backend cleanups, and a wave of new mode/unboxed-repr draft PRs.
repositories.new_features_summary: eval beta-reduction merged, mode-constraint-without-type syntax, CamlinternalQuote API expanded, ocamldep with-kind fix.
repositories.new_features:
- **`eval` beta-reduction in type expressions** is now supported: `<[ t ]> eval` reduces correctly with the new `Tquote_eval` type constructor, `reduce_head` in `Ctype`, and `new_quote_eval_ty`/`new_quote_ty`/`new_splice_ty` helpers in `Btype`. Two long-running metaprogramming PRs landed together: [#5323](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5323) (fix quote & splice typing, by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski)) and [#5402](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5402) (beta-reducing `eval`, by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski)).
- **Mode constraint syntax without a type annotation** is now accepted: `(x @ local)` is valid without spelling out the type. Documentation in `jane/doc/extensions/_05-modes/syntax.md` updated and legacy syntax removed from pprintast ([#5602](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5602), by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke)).
- **`CamlinternalQuote` API expanded**: `Identifier.eval` added to the stdlib-level quote identifier set; new `Vb_attribute` module exposed with `mk`; `let_rec_simple` and the module-binding constructor updated to accept `Vb_attribute.t list` for attribute propagation through quotations ([#5649](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5649), [#5650](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5650), [#5645](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5645), by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)).
- **`path_eval` and `add_runtime_metaprogramming_types`** added to `Predef` to populate `expr`/`eval` only when `Runtime_metaprogramming` is enabled, avoiding unintended availability in ordinary builds ([#5402](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5402)).
- **`with kind` annotations now scanned by `ocamldep`**: the dependency analysis tool correctly records dependencies introduced by `with kind_` constraints so incremental builds stay sound ([#5628](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5628), by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin)).
- **x86 binary emitter completeness**: missing instruction encodings added and error messages improved, fixing an ASAN failure triggered by `Camlinternaleval` ([#5653](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5653), by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)).
- **Camlinternaleval compiled at `-O3`**: the JIT-based eval library now uses the highest optimisation level, bringing it in line with other performance-critical compiler libraries ([#5646](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5646), by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)).
- **Tail-call stack-overflow move fixed in the IRC register allocator**: a move instruction emitted for stack-overflow handling at tail-call sites was placed incorrectly, causing potential corruption; now resolved ([#5591](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5591), by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc)).
- **Debuginfo.to_location improved**: location information used in zero-alloc error messages is now derived more accurately from `Debuginfo.t`, improving the quality of warning 55 diagnostics ([#5444](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5444), by [Guillaume Bury](https://github.com/Gbury)).
- **IRC spilling heuristic `Set_choose` removed** from `Spilling_heuristics.t`: the unused variant is deleted, simplifying the register-allocator API ([#5637](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5637), by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc)).
- **Reaper bug fixes**: two separate defects corrected — wrong argument count for unboxed callees ([#5633](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5633)) and a crash when loading a mutable variable with no source from an unboxed value ([#5655](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5655)), both by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs).
- **flambda2 type system: `is_null` classified as naked**: the null-pointer check predicate is now typed as a naked integer operation rather than a boxed-value operation, fixing a latent type mismatch in flambda2 ([#5642](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5642), by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)).
- **`native_code` flag set in the fexpr compiler**: the fexpr (flambda expression) compiler now correctly signals native code mode, fixing a flag mismatch that could affect code generation ([#5647](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5647), by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)).
- **CamlinternalQuote pretty-printer bug fixes (round 2)**: seven parenthesisation bugs fixed — assert/lazy arguments, `PatVariant` arguments, match/try case RHS, sequence elements, if-else branches, unboxed field subexpressions, and function constraint position ([#5660](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5660), by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)).
repositories.activity_summary: 34 new PRs opened covering mode bitfields, unboxed reprs, staging+GADTs, x86 optimizations, and a fresh Multicore domains fix.
repositories.activity:
- **New metaprogramming work opened**: [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) opened a two-part staging+GADTs series — [#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641) tracks the environment stage through type-checking and adds exhaustive GADT annotation tests, and [#5586](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5586) (updated on the parent) makes spliced types non-instantiable to close a soundness hole. He also opened [#5670](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5670) to support mode annotations under quotes (previously silently dropped), [#5674](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5674) to raise an error instead of silently dropping jkind annotations in quotes, and [#5643](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5643) on local constraints not time-travelling through staging.
- **Mode system expansion PRs**: [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) opened a four-part mode-bitfields series ([#5671](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5671) abstraction barriers, [#5672](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5672) constant mode product packing, [#5673](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5673) modality/crossing packing, [#5677](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5677) comonadic fragment packing) to eliminate allocation in the constant mode representation. [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) opened [#5666](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5666) adding `poisoned` and `poisoning` modes as contention/portability analogues of `write`/`observable`, and his earlier [#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608) (`write`/`observable` mode diamond) continued receiving review discussion.
- **New unboxed-repr and layout work**: [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) opened [#5678](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5678) (draft) adding constructor-level `[@repr null/immediate/pointer/value/unboxed]` annotations for allocation-free variant encodings, [#5667](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5667) (draft) fixing recursive unboxed product dummy layout shapes, [#5665](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5665) adding local abstract kind substitutions, and [#5668](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5668) enabling user-defined `[@@or_null]` type declarations with custom constructor names.
- **x86 backend optimization PRs opened**: [ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) opened [#5635](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5635) replacing spill/reload pairs around calls with push/pop instructions (shorter encoding, reduced frame size, gated by `-cfg-push-pop-around-calls`), [#5639](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5639) adding x86 peephole optimizations, and [#5651](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5651) adding an optimization-fuel infrastructure with a Python bisect driver for isolating miscompilations.
- **Multicore and runtime new PRs**: [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) opened [#5638](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5638) to change `Multicore` to keep domains alive; [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) opened [#5644](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5644) adding `Gc.Memprof.participate_globally` and [#5640](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5640) making `Atomic.make_contended` work on runtime4.
- **Small int / machtype work**: [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) opened [#5664](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5664) (draft) adding small-int machtype support and [#5676](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5676) adding codegen tests for 8-bit and 16-bit integers, complementing the ongoing [#5425](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5425) draft stopping sign-extension of unboxed small ints.
- **Register allocator improvements opened**: [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) opened [#5656](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5656) (early draft) using leaf-function clobber information to reduce spills, [#5659](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5659) for architecture-dependent spilling costs, and [#5632](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5632) to replace CFG dumps with a `Printcfg` module.
- **5.4 merge progress**: [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) continued advancing [#5598](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5598) (merge 5.4 runtime, now compiling and linking successfully); [#5487](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5487) (parsing conflicts) was merged by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js).
- **Ongoing mode-solver rewrite**: [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) continued review discussions on the mode-variable-levels chain ([#5478](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5478), [#5479](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5479), [#5480](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5480), [#5481](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5481)) with review from [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs).
- **Zero-alloc on function parameters**: [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian) continued iterating on [#5493](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5493) with review from [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin), addressing arity inference, backend test coverage, and signature matching for `zero_alloc` parameter annotations.
- **Closed without merge**: Several old test-baseline PRs from [Nick Roberts](https://github.com/ncik-roberts) ([#2537](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/2537), [#3163](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3163), [#3171](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3171), [#3175](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3175), [#3176](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3176), [#3239](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3239)) and [#5569](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5569) (remove legacy mode syntax from AST) were closed.
repositories.discussions_summary: Mode diamond design (write/observable/poisoned axes), Patricia tree optimization strategy, and eval beta-reduction safety drew extended review threads.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **`write`/`observable` mode diamond design** in [#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608) produced an extended exchange among [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke), [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere), and [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) on whether to expose the diamond vs. two separate axes to users, modality interactions, and implications. [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) requested comprehensive documentation of all implied modes after discovering undocumented `stateful => nonportable` and `read_write => uncontended` implications.
- **Optimized Patricia trees** in [#5627](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5627): [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) and [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) questioned the removal of `Set`/`Map` abstraction sharing, raised concerns about complexity guarantees for `union_shared`/`diff_shared`, and asked [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) to split the PR to isolate the `pbit` packing from the inlining changes so the flambda2 optimizer's gaps can be diagnosed separately.
- **ARM64 branch relaxation** in [#5499](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5499): [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) provided a detailed Claude-assisted second-pass review identifying five subtle issues including dead `instr_size` code, stale `stack_offset` during relaxation, reversed emission order in `List.rev_map`, a misleading comment about `relaxed_instruction_size`, and a shared `num_stack_slots` hash table. [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) addressed several of these in followup commits.
- **`poisoned`/`poisoning` mode safety** in [#5666](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5666): [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) asked whether writing to `poisoned` fields is safe given OCaml's bounded data-race guarantees. [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) confirmed that data races only corrupt the specific locations involved, leaving the rest of program execution unaffected.
repositories.trends_summary: Metaprogramming infrastructure is converging, mode system is expanding with new lattice shapes, and backend optimization work is intensifying.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Metaprogramming infrastructure maturing**: a sustained series of PRs — quote/splice typing fixes ([#5323](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5323)), `eval` beta-reduction ([#5402](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5402)), attribute propagation ([#5650](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5650)), Lregion plumbing ([#5645](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5645)), and CamlinternalQuote reviews and bug fixes ([#5649](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5649), [#5660](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5660)) — all merged in one week, indicating the staging/quoting subsystem is approaching a usable baseline.
- **Mode system lattice complexity growing**: new `write`/`observable` ([#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608)), `poisoned`/`poisoning` ([#5666](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5666)), and a mode bitfields packing series ([#5671](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5671)–[#5677](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5677)) opened in the same week signal that the mode lattice is actively expanding and its in-memory representation is being overhauled for performance.
- **x86 backend low-level optimization push**: push/pop spill replacement ([#5635](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5635)), peephole passes ([#5639](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5639)), and leaf-function clobber tracking ([#5656](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5656)) all opened in the same week, suggesting a focused effort to reduce generated code size and register pressure on amd64.
- **Unboxed representation generalization**: constructor-level `[@repr ...]` annotations ([#5678](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5678)), custom `[@@or_null]` types ([#5668](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5668)), and recursive unboxed product layout fixes ([#5667](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5667)) point toward a general push to let users control allocation-free representations directly in source code.
- **5.4 upstream merge progressing incrementally**: parsing conflicts resolved ([#5487](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5487)), runtime compilation working ([#5598](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5598)), and the overall upstream 5.4 merge ([#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325)) tracking steadily.
repositories.contributors_summary: 22 contributors merged or opened PRs, with Mark Shinwell leading metaprogramming merges and Jules Jacobs opening a wave of draft mode/unboxed PRs.
repositories.contributors:

repositories.brief_summary: Upstream compiler sync merging 5.2.0minus-34, adding layout polymorphism and parser updates.
repositories.new_features_summary: New `layout_` syntax for locally abstract layouts, polymorphic sort variables, and updated block access syntax.
repositories.new_features:
- Added **`Ptyp_newlayout` AST node** supporting `layout_ a b c. T` syntax for introducing locally abstract layouts into scope, with corresponding `LAYOUT` token and `lpoly` grammar rule in the parser ([#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225))
- Introduced **polymorphic sort variables** (`Genvar`) in `jkind_intf.ml`, including new `Sort` interface operations: `new_genvar`, `is_genvar`, `sub_with`, `instance_with`, `instance`, `to_string_genvar`, and `print_with_genvars` for layout polymorphism support ([#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225))
- Added **`Layout_poly_coercion` error type** in `includecore.mli` with variants `Extra_lhs`, `Extra_rhs`, `Instantiate_lhs_to_rhs`, and `Instantiate_lhs` to report mismatches in layout-polymorphic primitive coercions ([#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225))
- Removed `Baccess_array` from the **block access AST**, consolidating array indexing syntax; unboxed integer index variants (`.L(E)`, `.l(E)`, `.S(E)`, `.s(E)`, `.n(E)`) are no longer parsed as `Baccess_array` ([#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225))
- Extended **Merlin's ignored compiler flags** list to include `-dtlambda`, `-O4`, `-cfg-merge-blocks`, `-no-cfg-merge-blocks`, `-cfg-value-propagation-flow`, `-no-cfg-value-propagation-flow`, and `-reaper-max-unbox-size`, preventing these new upstream flags from causing Merlin errors ([#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225))
repositories.activity_summary: Upstream compiler sync via PR #225 merging 5.2.0minus-34 changes across 163 files.
repositories.activity: - **Upstream compiler sync** merged via [#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923), incorporating 5.2.0minus-34 changes across 163 files with 78,226 additions and 58,706 deletions; described as a straightforward merge with conflict resolution in a single commit
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing synchronisation with upstream OxCaml compiler to track layout polymorphism and jkind axis features.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Layout polymorphism** continues to evolve in the upstream compiler, with this sync introducing generalized sort variables and the `layout_` binder syntax, reflecting an active development effort in the OxCaml fork around kind-indexed type systems ([#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225))
- **AST simplification** is visible in the removal of `Baccess_array` variants, suggesting the upstream compiler is consolidating its block access representation ahead of further unboxed array work ([#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225))
repositories.contributors_summary: Liam Stevenson authored and merged the upstream compiler sync PR.
repositories.contributors: - [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened and merged [#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225), performing the 5.2.0minus-34 upstream sync including conflict resolution across the parser, type checker, and analysis modules

repositories.brief_summary: Two new issues opened around API usability: a race condition in Eio.Path.load and a proposal to make buffer_sink closable.
repositories.activity_summary: Two new issues raised by Volker Diels-Grabsch covering a Path.load race condition and a buffer_sink closability proposal.
repositories.activity:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) opened [#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) reporting that **`Eio.Path.load` has a race condition** caused by stat-then-read sequencing, and also fails on `/proc` files where the kernel reports size 0 at stat time; the proposal is to use file size only as a hint for the initial buffer size and otherwise behave like `Eio.Flow.read_all`.
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) opened [#834](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/834) proposing that **`Eio.Flow.buffer_sink`** be made closable so it can be passed to functions that close the flow, raising open questions about whether `close` should be a no-op or raise on subsequent writes, and whether the change is backwards-compatible.
- Long-standing issue [#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/382) on **making `Waiters` lock-free** (originally opened by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) in 2022) received a comment from [Mitesh Sharma](https://github.com/Miteshryp) asking whether a CQS-based implementation has been completed as part of coursework supervised by `@kayceesrk`.
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussion around Eio.Path.load race condition on /proc files and a proposal to add close support to buffer_sink.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) highlights a **`Eio.Path.load` race condition** when files change between `stat` and `read`, which also causes silent empty-string returns for `/proc` virtual files; [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) includes a concrete reproducer showing `"/proc/sys/fs/file-nr"` returning `""` via Eio while `cat` returns the expected content.
- [#834](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/834) proposes extending **`Eio.Flow.buffer_sink`** with a `Close` capability, opening design questions about API backwards-compatibility and the semantics of closing an in-memory buffer sink.
- [#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/382) sees renewed interest in the **lock-free `Waiters`** proposal from a student group implementing CQS as a course project, pointing to ongoing community engagement with Eio's internal synchronization primitives.
repositories.trends_summary: Community contributors are surfacing API usability gaps around file I/O and flow abstractions.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Two separate issues opened by the same contributor ([Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog)) in a single week suggest **growing external usage uncovering edge cases** in `Eio.Path` and `Eio.Flow` APIs, particularly around `/proc` file handling and composability with close-aware consumers ([#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835), [#834](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/834)).
- Student engagement with [#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/382) indicates academic interest in **lock-free synchronization primitives** within Eio, with coursework implementations potentially contributing back to the project.
repositories.contributors_summary: Volker Diels-Grabsch opened two new issues; Mitesh Sharma commented on a long-standing lock-free waiters issue.
repositories.contributors:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) opened two issues this week: [#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) reporting the `Eio.Path.load` race condition with `/proc` files, and [#834](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/834) proposing `close` support for `Eio.Flow.buffer_sink`.
- [Mitesh Sharma](https://github.com/Miteshryp) commented on [#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/382) inquiring about the status of CQS implementation in Eio as part of a course project.

group_summaries.brief_summary: Dune 3.22.0 release, OCaml 4.14.3 LTS security fix, and Windows/MSYS2 improvements drove ecosystem activity.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: OCaml 4.14.3 LTS (CVE-2026-28364 fix), parseff (algebraic-effect parsers), raven 1.0.0~alpha3 (scientific computing), and jws 0.0.1 released.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **OCaml 4.14.3 LTS release page** added to ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3571)), marking it as the current LTS and documenting the **CVE-2026-28364** security fix (Marshal deserialization buffer over-read, OSEC-2026-01), plus backports for [ocaml/ocaml#12207](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12207), [ocaml/ocaml#13430](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13430), [ocaml/ocaml#13448](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13448), and [ocaml/ocaml#14007](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14007)
- **[parseff 0.1.0 and 0.2.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29526)** merged — direct-style parser combinator library for OCaml 5 using algebraic effects, with zero-copy span APIs and domain-safe operation; 0.2.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29573](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29573)) unified `*1` combinators with an `~at_least` parameter
- **[raven 1.0.0~alpha3](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29490)** merged — 11-package scientific computing suite including `nx-oxcaml` (pure-OCaml SIMD tensor backend) and `kaun-board` (TUI training dashboard), with a unified `Nx.t`/`Rune.t` tensor type
- **[jws 0.0.1](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29557)** merged — RFC 7515 JWS implementation using jsont and mirage-crypto; later used as a dependency in letsencrypt 2.0.0
- **[dnsrobot 0.1.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29541)** merged — OCaml client for DNS Robot providing DNS lookups, WHOIS, SSL checks, and 50+ network tools via an Lwt-based async API
- **[dune-ai-context 0.1.2](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29536)** merged — extracts dune external dependencies and generates interface context for AI-assisted coding tools
- **[tm-grammars 1.1.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29548)** merged — adds TextMate grammar definitions for 200+ languages as OCaml strings, replacing the broken 1.0.0 tarball
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **[dune 3.22.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29562)** released to opam-repository with 18 packages, featuring extensive build tracing, expanded sandboxing defaults, and oxcaml `.cms` file support; a companion announcement PR ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3570](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3570)) is awaiting merge on ocaml.org
- **OCaml 4.14.3 LTS** published on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3571)) with a CVE-2026-28364 security fix and several backported bug fixes from OCaml 5.x
- **Windows/MSYS2 support** received sustained attention with four new issues ([ocaml/opam-repository#29575](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29575), [#29576](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29576), [#29577](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29577), [#29578](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29578)) covering missing MSYS2 depext declarations and UCRT64 migration needs
- **MirageOS ecosystem modernisation** progressed: [letsencrypt 2.0.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29572) drops yojson, [mirage-runtime](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29571) relaxes the lwt < 6 constraint, and [charrua 3.1.1](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29538) fixes DHCP cancellation semantics
- **[atd 4.0.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29552)** released with 11 packages, introducing experimental module support and the new `atdml` code generator as successor to `atdgen`
- **[ocamlformat 0.29.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29553)** released with OCaml 5.5 syntax support, Odoc 3.0 parser integration, and a new `letop-punning` option
- **Planet content** on ocaml.org expanded with automated scrapes merging posts from tunbury, cwn, and anil across PRs [ocaml/ocaml.org#3566](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3566)–[#3569](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3569) and [#3573](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3573)
group_summaries.activity_summary: Dune 3.22 triggered upper-bound fixes across stdune, dune-rpc, and ocamlformat; lwt 6 compatibility work spans 84 packages; markdown lint fixes pending on ocaml.org.
group_summaries.activity:
- **[dune 3.22.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29562)** release required coordinated upper-bound fixes: [stdune](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29561) and [dune-rpc](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29555) updated for breaking API changes; [ocamlformat constraints on dune 3.22](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29568) pending due to changed `(diff)` rule behaviour
- **[mirage-crypto 2.1.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29545)** merged with 7 packages — exposes `Mirage_crypto_ec.Dsa.Primitive` for NIST curve primitives useful for protocols like SPAKE2
- **[Gospel 0.3.1](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29559)** merged — OCaml specification language update for ppxlib and cmdliner compatibility
- **[elpi 3.6.2](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29558)** merged — fixes quadratic growth in program signature storage in the embeddable λProlog interpreter
- **[xapi-backtrace 0.8](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29542)** merged — fixes backtrace loss on reraise and adds regression tests
- **[camlpdf 2.9 and cpdf 2.9](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29546)** merged — PDF library and command-line tools update
- **[charrua-client 3.0.0 and 3.1.0 marked `avoid-version`](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29570)** due to MirageOS network stack runtime issues
- **[tm-grammars 1.0.0 marked unavailable](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29554)** due to broken release tarball; replaced by 1.1.0
- **Open PRs awaiting review:** [ppxlib and ppxlib-tools 0.38.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29563) (OCaml 5.5 support, bivariant type parameters); [Ortac 0.8.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29564) (QCheck-STM+Domains parallel testing); [lsp 1.26.0-5.5~preview](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29565) (destruct custom request, non-dune build system support); [gendarme 0.4.1](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29566) (9 packages, ppxlib>=0.36); [alcobar 0.3](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29556) (Alcotest-compatible fuzzing); [oniguruma 0.2.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29583) (new syntaxes and search options)
- **[ocaml/opam-repository#29530](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29530)** — [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) continues broad lwt 6 compatibility work across 84 packages
- **Markdown lint fixes** for 4.14.x release data files opened on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3572](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3572)) by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac), resolving MD003, MD004, MD012, MD026, and MD040 violations — awaiting merge
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Dune 3.22 coordinated release**: [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) led the dune 3.22.0 release in opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#29562](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29562)) and simultaneously opened the release announcement on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3570](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3570)), representing a coordinated release across both repositories
- **OCaml 4.14.3 LTS publication**: The new LTS release page on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3571)) documents fixes for upstream compiler issues ([ocaml/ocaml#12207](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12207), [ocaml/ocaml#13430](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13430), [ocaml/ocaml#13448](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13448), [ocaml/ocaml#14007](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14007)), connecting the website content directly to compiler repository work
- **jws → letsencrypt dependency chain**: [ocaml/opam-repository#29557](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29557) (jws 0.0.1) was merged as a prerequisite for [ocaml/opam-repository#29572](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29572) (letsencrypt 2.0.0), illustrating a same-week dependency resolution in opam-repository
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **[MSYS2 depext gaps](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29575)** — [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) filed four issues ([ocaml/opam-repository#29575](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29575), [#29576](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29576), [#29577](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29577), [#29578](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29578)) covering missing MSYS2 depext declarations for conf-m4, conf-perl, and conf-findutils, and the need to support UCRT64 as MSYS2 deprecated MINGW64 in March 2026; companion PR [ocaml/opam-repository#29579](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29579) addresses all four with package-prefix variable interpolation
- **[New ocaml-option-clang-cl package](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29582)** — [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) proposes a marker package enabling MSVC clang-cl as the OCaml compiler's CC, following the pattern of ocaml-option-musl, affecting 16 compiler packages
- **[MirageOS lwt/dune dependency puzzle](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29569)** — [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) asked how to express conditional lwt version constraints depending on dune version; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) and [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) explained the disjunctive constraint pattern `"dune" {>= "3.22"} | "lwt" {< "6"}`, noting that the 0install solver gives slight priority to earlier disjunction terms
- **[Refine constraints on lwt 6](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29530)** — [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) continues broad lwt 6 compatibility work spanning 84 files; discussion covers using opam-health-check-ng to detect which packages need `lwt < 6`
- **[MetaOCaml unexpected solver selection](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/28249)** — ongoing discussion on solver heuristics where `ocaml-option-tsan` switch creation unexpectedly installs BER MetaOCaml variants; suggestion to add `flags: avoid-version` to MetaOCaml packages
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Windows/MSYS2 ecosystem support** is receiving focused attention: UCRT64 environment support for mingw-w64 packages ([ocaml/opam-repository#29578](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29578)), missing MSYS2 depext declarations ([ocaml/opam-repository#29575](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29575)–[#29577](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29577)), and the proposed `ocaml-option-clang-cl` marker package ([ocaml/opam-repository#29582](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29582)) — all driven by MSYS2's deprecation of MINGW64 in March 2026
- **Dune 3.22 ripple effects** — the major dune release required coordinated upper-bound fixes in stdune ([ocaml/opam-repository#29561](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29561)), dune-rpc ([ocaml/opam-repository#29555](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29555)), and ocamlformat ([ocaml/opam-repository#29568](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29568)), and triggered a release announcement on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3570](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3570))
- **OCaml 5 / algebraic effects adoption** — [parseff 0.1.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29526) demonstrates continued growth in algebraic-effects programming style; [raven's nx-oxcaml backend](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29490) shows pure-OCaml SIMD via OxCaml's unboxed types approaching C backend performance
- **MirageOS dependency modernisation** — [letsencrypt 2.0.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29572) drops yojson in favour of jws/jsont, [mirage-runtime](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29571) relaxes the lwt < 6 restriction when dune >= 3.22 is used, and [charrua buggy versions](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29570) are marked avoid-version
- **LTS maintenance alongside OCaml 5 transition** — the 4.14.3 LTS release page on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3571)) reflects continued investment in the OCaml 4.14 security track during the OCaml 5 migration period
- **Planet scrape failures** are appearing consistently: `ocamlpro`, `mirage`, `patricoferris`, and `dinosaure` regularly time out, while `emilpriver` returns 403 and `jonludlam` returns 404 ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3566](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3566)–[#3569](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3569), [#3573](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3573)), suggesting stale feed registrations needing cleanup

group_summaries.brief_summary: Dune 3.22.0 shipped; ocaml-lsp 1.26.0-5.5~preview released; js_of_ocaml variable coalescing cuts V8 register usage by up to 91%.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dune @revdep alias, ocaml-lsp destruct request and build-system config via env vars, opam PowerShell completions, js_of_ocaml variable coalescing.
group_summaries.new_features:
- [ocaml/dune#13800](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13800): New `@revdep`, `@revdep-check`, and `@revdep-runtest` aliases that build all reverse dependencies of libraries in a directory, useful when refactoring.
- [ocaml/dune#13876](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13876): `dune promote` extended to promote a path prefix and all descendants under `foo/`; a `--file` flag restores single-file behavior.
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583): New `destruct` custom LSP request exposing Merlin's destruct functionality, included in the `1.26.0-5.5~preview` release.
- Merlin build-system configuration via environment variables (`$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM`, `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_ROOT`) added in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp), enabling use with build systems other than Dune.
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1580): Improved `signatureHelp` readability for long signatures in the preview release.
- [ocaml/opam#6869](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6869): New PowerShell completion support added as part of the cmdliner 2.1.0 completion overhaul, replacing existing bash/zsh scripts (closes [ocaml/opam#6853](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6853)).
- [ocaml/opam#6867](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6867): Vendored cmdliner updated from 1.3.0 to 2.1.0, resolving the long-standing issue [ocaml/opam#684](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/684).
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166): New variable coalescing optimization pass (`--enable/--disable var-coalescing`) merging JS variables with disjoint lifespans, reducing V8 register usage by 28–91% on large programs.
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144): Wasm bigstring primitives optimized with a DataView workaround for non-V8 engines.
- [ocaml/merlin#2039](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2039): `PATH_MAX` portability fix in `ocamlmerlin.c` for GNU Hurd builds where the constant is not defined.
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1597](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1597): OCaml 5.5 parsetree adaptations in the `550-preview` branch — `Pexp_letmodule` replaced by `Pexp_struct_item` across `folding_range.ml`, `semantic_highlighting.ml`, and `action_extract.ml`.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- 8 of 10 repositories saw activity this week; [ocaml-dune/spawn](https://github.com/ocaml-dune/spawn) had no recorded commits or issues.
- [Dune 3.22.0](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13769) was released on March 18 via [ocaml/dune#13769](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13769), followed by a wave of cache-consolidation and Windows-compatibility merges.
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) published `1.26.0-5.5~preview` with OCaml 5.5 parsetree adaptations, a new `destruct` custom request ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583)), and merlin build-system configuration via environment variables.
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) merged a variable coalescing optimization pass ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166)) reducing V8 register usage by 28–91%, alongside Wasm bigstring improvements ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144)).
- [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) received a four-PR series modernizing its completion system with cmdliner 2.1.0 and adding PowerShell support ([ocaml/opam#6869](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6869)).
- OCaml 5.5 compatibility is the dominant cross-cutting theme: [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened tracking issues in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1596](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1596), [ocaml/odoc#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401), and [ocaml/ocamlfind#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115), while [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195) opened a 12,000-line draft adaption.
- OxCaml integration work is active in both [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) ([ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399), [ocaml/odoc#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404)) and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)).
group_summaries.activity_summary: Cache consolidation in dune, Wasm/OCaml 5.5 work in js_of_ocaml, opam legacy removal, and ocaml-lsp/dune compatibility fixes dominate ongoing work.
group_summaries.activity:
- [ocaml/dune#13840](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13840) merged `dune_cache_storage` into `dune_cache`, removing the redundant module split; [ocaml/dune#13854](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13854) merged `dune_rpc_private` into `dune_rpc`, promoting internal `.mli` files to the public API surface.
- [ocaml/dune#13884](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13884) fixed incorrect value sharing when serialising the targets digest database; [ocaml/dune#13883](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13883) wrapped `Stdlib.Marshal` in a safer interface with an explicit `~sharing` parameter.
- [ocaml/dune#13856](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13856) fixed Windows binary lookup by stripping `.exe` from map keys (fixes [ocaml/dune#12433](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12433)); [ocaml/dune#13838](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13838) used `open_out_bin` in the configure step to avoid CRLF line endings on Windows.
- [ocaml/dune#13864](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13864) fixes `.exe` handling in `.install` files on Windows (fixes [ocaml/dune#12430](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12430)); [ocaml/dune#13833](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13833) by [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) fixes a spurious dependency cycle with `enabled_if` and `%{read:...}` when a `package` stanza is present.
- [ocaml/dune#13844](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13844) adds CI jobs to check per-package dependency specifications and lower bounds for each `.opam` package, addressing [ocaml/dune#13731](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13731) and [ocaml/dune#13821](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13821).
- [ocaml/dune#13875](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13875) moves per-target digest storage into the rule cache, removing reliance on the stat cache for build targets.
- [ocaml/dune#13885](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13885) begins explicit patch-back support for dune 3.23, following [ocaml/dune#13882](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13882) which documents the currently broken state of sandboxed promotion.
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1594) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) fixes compilation with Dune 3.22; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) noted dependency constraints also need updating before merge.
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) `dune.ml` updated to use new `Drpc.Request.Id.make` and `Drpc.Request.Initialize.create` APIs with the `Chan` module refactored to separate `close` and `stop` operations ([e5d0d90a](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/commit/e5d0d90a7e4c7cacc9aac029b6c58f457791604d)).
- [ocaml/merlin#2047](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2047): ocamlformat upgrade to 0.29.0 opened by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos), under review; [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) raised questions about reformatting scope and opam package availability.
- [ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625) (long-running HTTP repository performance WIP by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)) received a fix from [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) for an infinite loop in tar stream handling.
- [ocaml/opam#6866](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6866) removes the decade-old `Num` patches fallback in `OpamDarcs` (dead code since darcs 2.10.3 in 2016); [ocaml/opam#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862) fixes `GetCurrentProcessToken` availability in MSYS2 builds by setting `_WIN32_WINNT` to Windows 8.1.
- [ocaml/opam#6854](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6854) and [ocaml/opam#6868](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6868) complete the compatibility and argument-definition work for the cmdliner 2.1.0 completion overhaul by [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian).
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2201](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2201) and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2200](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2200) merged — fixes for edge cases in variable coalescing (empty try bodies) and inline duplication exposed by [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2198](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2198).
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187) (Wasm dynlink/toplevel unification by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)) and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189) (Wasm native effects via Stack Switching) are active with ongoing review.
- [ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399) (upstream OxCaml integration by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w)) received a review request directed at [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam); draft [ocaml/odoc#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404) opened as a follow-up fixing missing parentheses in polymorphic parameter rendering.
- [ocaml/opam-file-format#65](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format/issues/65): README update by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) fixing [ocaml/opam-file-format#64](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format/issues/64) remains open awaiting merge.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **OCaml 5.5 toolchain readiness**: [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened coordinated tracking issues across [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1596](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1596), [ocaml/odoc#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401), and [ocaml/ocamlfind#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115); the polymorphic parameters feature upstreamed in [ocaml/ocaml#13806](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13806) drives both [ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399) and [ocaml/odoc#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404), while [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195) represents a 12,000-line 5.5 adaptation for js_of_ocaml.
- **Dune 3.22.0 API changes affecting ocaml-lsp**: the `dune_rpc` API changes in the 3.22.0 release required [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1594) to restore compilation; discussion in [ocaml/dune#13674](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13674) additionally noted that `stdune` breaking changes in 3.22 affect ocaml-lsp-server, with `stdune` potentially being made private in future releases.
- **OxCaml integration across documentation and compilation tooling**: [ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399) and [ocaml/odoc#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404) add OxCaml AST support to the documentation generator, while [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105) adds OxCaml support to the compiler; both use `cppo` preprocessor directives for conditional compilation.
- **Merlin/ocaml-lsp issue deduplication**: [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1586](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1586) (locate command incorrectly returning 'already at origin') was closed by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) in favour of its Merlin counterpart [ocaml/merlin#2033](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2033), where [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) provided a code-level analysis pointing to `src/analysis/locate.ml#L835-L859`.
- **js_of_ocaml Wasm native effects blocked on Dune**: [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189) (Wasm Stack Switching effects) is blocked on [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177) and [ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613), illustrating a direct dependency from js_of_ocaml's Wasm backend on Dune infrastructure.
- **ocaml-lsp preview branch pins merlin pre-release**: the `1.26.0-5.5~preview` branch in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) pins `merlin-lib` to `5.7-505~preview` and requires `dune-rpc >= 3.22.0`, coupling the preview release track tightly to pre-release versions of both Merlin and Dune.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [ocaml/dune#13871](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13871): Dune 3.22.0 fails to bootstrap on Windows 7 because `GetSystemTimePreciseAsFileTime` (introduced in Windows 8) is now referenced; [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) is preparing a compatibility fallback.
- [ocaml/dune#13844](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13844): Debate on whether the new CI per-package dependency-check jobs should run on every PR or only on merges to `main`, given 20–30 minute job times.
- [ocaml/dune#13832](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13832): [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) reported a spurious dependency cycle when a library uses `enabled_if` with `%{read:...}` and a `package` directive; fix submitted in [ocaml/dune#13833](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13833).
- [ocaml/ocamlfind#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115): [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) reported that OCaml 5.5 users calling `ocamlfind ocamlopt` see duplicate warnings (`Cannot read directory ../stublibs`, `Cannot read directory ./stublibs`) on every invocation; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) cc'd as subject-matter expert.
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1209](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1209): [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) and [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) investigated whether `dune-rpc` emits `Progress.Success` before build artifacts are readable, as a potential cause of spurious `Unbound module` diagnostics; Rudi noted existing RPC diagnostic bugs but considered the timing explanation unlikely.
- [ocaml/opam#6454](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6454): [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) asked how to set solver criteria via the OCaml API using `OpamSwitchCommand.create`; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) clarified that `OpamSolverConfig.update ~solver_preferences_default:(...)` is the correct approach for opam-solver 2.5.0.
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189): [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) and [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) discussed the removal of `--experimental-wasm-stack-switching` and `--experimental-wasm-imported-strings` flags in Node 26; [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) proposed maintaining a per-Node-version documentation table of required Wasm feature flags.
- [ocaml/merlin#2033](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2033): [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) provided a detailed code-level analysis explaining why `locate` incorrectly returns 'already at origin' when the cursor is on a different type declaration, pointing to the heuristic in `src/analysis/locate.ml#L835-L859`.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **OCaml 5.5 toolchain-wide compatibility** is the most active cross-cutting theme: coordinated tracking issues in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1596](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1596), [ocaml/odoc#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401), [ocaml/ocamlfind#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115), and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195) indicate a concerted push to prepare the tooling ecosystem before 5.5 reaches a stable release.
- **Windows and cross-platform hardening**: multiple Windows fixes merged in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) ([ocaml/dune#13856](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13856), [ocaml/dune#13838](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13838), [ocaml/dune#13864](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13864)) alongside new PowerShell completions in [ocaml/opam#6869](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6869) and the MSYS2 build fix in [ocaml/opam#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862) reflect sustained post-3.22.0 investment in Windows support across the toolchain.
- **OxCaml integration expanding into the ecosystem**: parallel work in [ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399) and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105) signals that OxCaml support is moving beyond the core compiler into documentation and compilation tools.
- **JavaScript output quality and Wasm capability expansion in js_of_ocaml**: variable coalescing ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166)) builds on constant sinking ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2167](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2167)) and assignment operators to form a series of complementary output-quality improvements, while [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189), and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831) push the Wasm backend toward feature parity with the JS backend.
- **Dune internal consolidation reducing module count**: the merge of `dune_cache_storage` into `dune_cache` ([ocaml/dune#13840](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13840)) and `dune_rpc_private` into `dune_rpc` ([ocaml/dune#13854](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13854)), combined with the safer `Stdlib.Marshal` wrapper ([ocaml/dune#13883](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13883)) and CI package-check additions ([ocaml/dune#13844](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13844)), represent sustained investment in correctness and maintainability following the 3.22.0 release.
- **Preview release track adoption**: the `1.26.0-5.5~preview` release in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) and the ongoing `550-preview` branch work reflect a strategy of early compatibility validation against pre-release OCaml versions, reducing the integration burden when stable releases arrive.

group_summaries.brief_summary: Active week in oxcaml/oxcaml with metaprogramming and mode system merges; oxcaml/merlin synced upstream compiler with layout polymorphism.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: eval beta-reduction, mode constraint syntax, CamlinternalQuote API expansions, reaper fixes, and Merlin layout polymorphism sync.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **`eval` beta-reduction in type expressions** now supported via new `Tquote_eval` type constructor and `reduce_head` in `Ctype`; landed via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5323](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5323) (quote & splice typing, by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski)) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5402](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5402) (beta-reducing `eval`, by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski)).
- **Mode constraint syntax without a type annotation** accepted — `(x @ local)` now valid without spelling out the type; documentation updated and legacy syntax removed from pprintast ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5602](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5602), by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke)).
- **`CamlinternalQuote` API expanded**: `Identifier.eval` added, new `Vb_attribute` module exposed with `mk`, and `let_rec_simple` updated to accept `Vb_attribute.t list` for attribute propagation through quotations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5649](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5649), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5650](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5650), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5645](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5645), by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)).
- **`with kind` annotations now scanned by `ocamldep`**: dependency analysis correctly records dependencies from `with kind_` constraints for sound incremental builds ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5628](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5628), by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin)).
- **x86 binary emitter completeness**: missing instruction encodings added and error messages improved, fixing an ASAN failure triggered by `Camlinternaleval` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5653](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5653), by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)).
- **Camlinternaleval compiled at `-O3`**: JIT-based eval library now uses the highest optimisation level ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5646](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5646), by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)).
- **Tail-call stack-overflow move fixed** in the IRC register allocator ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5591](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5591), by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc)).
- **`Debuginfo.to_location` improved** for more accurate zero-alloc (warning 55) error locations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5444](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5444), by [Guillaume Bury](https://github.com/Gbury)).
- **Reaper bug fixes**: wrong argument count for unboxed callees ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5633](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5633)) and crash when loading a mutable variable from an unboxed value with no source ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5655](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5655)), both by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)).
- **flambda2: `is_null` classified as naked integer** operation, fixing a latent type mismatch ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5642](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5642), by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)).
- **`native_code` flag set in the fexpr compiler**, fixing a flag mismatch that could affect code generation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5647](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5647), by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)).
- **CamlinternalQuote pretty-printer bug fixes**: seven parenthesisation bugs fixed across assert/lazy arguments, `PatVariant` arguments, match/try case RHS, sequence elements, if-else branches, unboxed field subexpressions, and function constraint position ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5660](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5660), by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)).
- **Merlin upstream sync** incorporating 5.2.0minus-34: new `Ptyp_newlayout` AST node for `layout_ a b c. T` syntax, polymorphic sort variables (`Genvar`), `Layout_poly_coercion` error type, and updated block access AST ([oxcaml/merlin#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225), by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)).
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **[oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)** had a high-volume week: multiple metaprogramming PRs merged (quote/splice typing, eval beta-reduction, CamlinternalQuote API), mode system expansion (new constraint syntax, mode diamond work), reaper and flambda2 bug fixes, and 34 new PRs opened covering mode bitfields, unboxed reprs, x86 backend optimizations, and Multicore runtime changes.
- **[oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin)** merged one large upstream compiler sync PR ([oxcaml/merlin#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225)) incorporating 5.2.0minus-34 changes across 163 files, adding layout polymorphism AST nodes and updating ignored compiler flags.
- **[oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository)**, **[oxcaml/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/js_of_ocaml)**, and **[janestreet/opam-repository](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository)** had no recorded activity this week.
group_summaries.activity_summary: 34 new PRs opened in oxcaml/oxcaml covering mode bitfields, unboxed reprs, x86 optimizations, and Multicore; Merlin synced upstream compiler.
group_summaries.activity:
- **New metaprogramming work opened in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)**: [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) opened a staging+GADTs series — [oxcaml/oxcaml#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641) (environment stage tracking and GADT annotation tests) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5586](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5586) (spliced types non-instantiable) — plus [oxcaml/oxcaml#5670](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5670) (mode annotations under quotes), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5674](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5674) (error on dropped jkind annotations), and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5643](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5643) (local constraints and staging).
- **Mode system expansion PRs**: [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) opened a four-part mode-bitfields series ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5671](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5671), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5672](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5672), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5673](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5673), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5677](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5677)) eliminating allocation in constant mode representation. [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) opened [oxcaml/oxcaml#5666](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5666) adding `poisoned`/`poisoning` modes; his `write`/`observable` mode diamond PR [oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608) continued receiving review.
- **Unboxed repr and layout work**: [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) opened [oxcaml/oxcaml#5678](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5678) (constructor-level `[@repr ...]` annotations), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5668](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5668) (user-defined `[@@or_null]` type declarations), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5667](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5667) (recursive unboxed product dummy layout fix), and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5665](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5665) (local abstract kind substitutions).
- **x86 backend optimization PRs**: [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) opened [oxcaml/oxcaml#5635](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5635) (push/pop spill replacement), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5639](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5639) (x86 peephole optimizations), and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5651](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5651) (optimization-fuel infrastructure with Python bisect driver).
- **Register allocator improvements**: [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) opened [oxcaml/oxcaml#5656](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5656) (leaf-function clobber tracking), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5659](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5659) (architecture-dependent spilling costs), and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5632](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5632) (`Printcfg` module replacing ad-hoc CFG dumps).
- **Multicore and runtime**: [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) opened [oxcaml/oxcaml#5638](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5638) to keep domains alive in `Multicore`; [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) opened [oxcaml/oxcaml#5644](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5644) (`Gc.Memprof.participate_globally`) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5640](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5640) (`Atomic.make_contended` runtime4 compatibility).
- **Small-int machtype work**: [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) opened [oxcaml/oxcaml#5664](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5664) (small-int machtype support) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5676](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5676) (codegen tests for 8-bit and 16-bit integers).
- **5.4 upstream merge progress**: [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) advanced [oxcaml/oxcaml#5598](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5598) to successful compile and link; [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) merged parsing-conflict resolution [oxcaml/oxcaml#5487](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5487).
- **Ongoing reviews**: [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) continued review on the mode-variable-levels chain ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5478](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5478), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5479](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5479), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5480](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5480), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5481](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5481)); [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian) iterated on zero-alloc parameter annotations [oxcaml/oxcaml#5493](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5493) with review from [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin).
- **Merlin sync**: [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) merged [oxcaml/merlin#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225) incorporating 5.2.0minus-34 upstream compiler changes across 163 files (78,226 additions, 58,706 deletions).
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Merlin tracks oxcaml/oxcaml compiler evolution**: [oxcaml/merlin#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225) syncs the 5.2.0minus-34 compiler snapshot, incorporating layout polymorphism features (`layout_` binder syntax, `Ptyp_newlayout` AST node, polymorphic sort variables) that are actively developed in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) through PRs such as [oxcaml/oxcaml#5665](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5665) (local abstract kind substitutions) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5634](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5634) (`Texp_apply_layout`).
- **Reaper flags surfaced in Merlin**: the `-reaper-max-unbox-size` compiler flag introduced by reaper unboxing work in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) (related to [oxcaml/oxcaml#5633](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5633) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5655](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5655)) was added to Merlin's ignored-flags list in [oxcaml/merlin#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225) to prevent spurious Merlin errors.
- **New oxcaml/oxcaml backend flags propagate to Merlin**: `-dtlambda`, `-O4`, `-cfg-merge-blocks`, `-cfg-value-propagation-flow` and related flags added to Merlin's ignored list in [oxcaml/merlin#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225) reflect new compiler options developed in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) CFG and flambda2 work.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **`write`/`observable` mode diamond design** in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608): extended exchange among [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke), [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere), and [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) on whether to expose the mode diamond vs. two separate axes to users, modality interactions, and implied-mode documentation. [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) requested documentation of undocumented implications (`stateful => nonportable`, `read_write => uncontended`).
- **Optimized Patricia trees** in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5627](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5627): [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) and [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) questioned removal of `Set`/`Map` abstraction sharing and raised concerns about `union_shared`/`diff_shared` complexity; requested the PR be split to isolate `pbit` packing from inlining changes so flambda2 optimizer gaps can be diagnosed independently.
- **ARM64 branch relaxation** in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5499](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5499): [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) identified five subtle issues including dead `instr_size` code, stale `stack_offset` during relaxation, reversed emission order in `List.rev_map`, a misleading comment about `relaxed_instruction_size`, and a shared `num_stack_slots` hash table; [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) addressed several in followup commits.
- **`poisoned`/`poisoning` mode safety** in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5666](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5666): [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) asked whether writing to `poisoned` fields is safe under OCaml's bounded data-race guarantees; [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) confirmed data races only corrupt the specific locations involved.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Metaprogramming infrastructure converging**: PRs for quote/splice typing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5323](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5323)), `eval` beta-reduction ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5402](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5402)), attribute propagation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5650](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5650)), `Lregion` plumbing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5645](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5645)), and CamlinternalQuote fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5649](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5649), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5660](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5660)) all merged in one week, with new staging+GADTs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5641](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5641)) and mode-annotation-under-quotes work ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5670](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5670)) immediately following.
- **Mode system lattice actively expanding**: new `write`/`observable` axes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608)), `poisoned`/`poisoning` modes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5666](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5666)), and a four-part mode-bitfields packing series ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5671](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5671)–[oxcaml/oxcaml#5677](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5677)) all opened in the same week, indicating simultaneous work on the mode lattice's semantics and its in-memory representation.
- **x86 backend low-level optimization push**: push/pop spill replacement ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5635](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5635)), peephole passes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5639](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5639)), leaf-function clobber tracking ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5656](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5656)), and optimization-fuel tooling ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5651](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5651)) all opened in one week, suggesting a coordinated effort to reduce generated code size and register pressure on amd64.
- **Unboxed representation generalization in progress**: constructor-level `[@repr ...]` annotations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5678](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5678)), custom `[@@or_null]` types ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5668](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5668)), and recursive unboxed product layout fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5667](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5667)) point toward letting users control allocation-free representations in source code.
- **Layout polymorphism now tracked across repositories**: the `layout_` binder syntax and polymorphic sort variables developed in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) now appear in [oxcaml/merlin#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225), indicating the feature has reached a stage where tooling support is actively maintained in sync.
- **5.4 upstream merge advancing incrementally**: parsing conflicts resolved ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5487](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5487)), runtime compilation working ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5598](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5598)), with typing changes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5551](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5551)) and stdlib conflicts ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5616](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5616)) still in progress against the overall merge tracker [oxcaml/oxcaml#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325).

group_summaries.brief_summary: Atomic.Array, List.append_map/rev_append_map merged; GC improvements, type soundness fixes, and bytecode effects bug landed in ocaml/ocaml.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Added Atomic.Array, List.append_map/rev_append_map, Set/Map singleton utilities; deprecated Gc.stat; optimization hints kept in bytecode.
group_summaries.new_features:
- New **`Atomic.Array` module** added to stdlib with `make`, `init`, `length`, `get`/`set`, `exchange`, `compare_and_set`, and `fetch_and_add` (safe and unsafe variants); joint work by [@clef-men](https://github.com/clef-men) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) ([ocaml/ocaml#14380](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14380))
- New **`List.append_map`** and **`List.rev_append_map`** functions added to stdlib, combining map and append in a single tail-recursive-modulo-cons pass with half the allocations of the composed version; by [Sylvain Boilard](https://github.com/SylvainBoilard) and Hugo Heuzard ([ocaml/ocaml#13554](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13554))
- New **`Set.S.singleton_to_elt`** and **`Map.S.singleton_to_binding`** functions added to the Set and Map modules; by Vincent Laviron ([ocaml/ocaml#14121](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14121))
- **`Hashtbl.is_randomized`** result is now reflected in the `R` parameter of `Sys.runtime_parameters`; by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm), [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27), and [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) ([ocaml/ocaml#14292](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14292))
- **`Gc.stat`** is now deprecated; users should call `Gc.quick_stat` directly and explicitly call `Gc.full_major ()` beforehand if up-to-date counters are needed; by [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) ([ocaml/ocaml#12023](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12023))
- **Optimization hints from Lambda** are now kept in bytecode modules and executables (`.cmo`/`.cma`/`.cme`), making them available to Js_of_ocaml and Wasm_of_ocaml; by Jérôme Vouillon ([ocaml/ocaml#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13438))
- **Warning 16** (unerasable-optional-argument) check improved to correctly handle module-dependent functions in the presence of GADTs and first-class modules; by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) ([ocaml/ocaml#14512](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14512), [ocaml/ocaml#14626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14626))
- **Prefetch read/write distinction** now correctly distinguishes between prefetching for read vs. write access; pass `-mprfchw` or `-march=native` for maximum benefit on AMD64; by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) and [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) ([ocaml/ocaml#14570](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14570), [ocaml/ocaml#14616](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14616))
- **Type soundness fix** for `check_counter_example_pat` against polymorphic types that could previously cause segfaults; by Stephen Dolan and [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) ([ocaml/ocaml#14434](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14434), [ocaml/ocaml#14652](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14652))
- **Bytecode effects fix** for heap corruption in `reperform` when using `try Effect.perform with Effect.Unhandled` under deep effect handlers; by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) ([ocaml/ocaml#14644](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14644), [ocaml/ocaml#14647](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14647))
- **`Float.Array.sub`/`append`/`concat`** bug fixed where these functions could return a value not equal to `[||]` for empty arrays; by Marc Lasson ([ocaml/ocaml#14635](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14635))
- **UTF-8 normalization** and un/capitalization functions optimized, both within and outside ASCII range; by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) ([ocaml/ocaml#14618](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14618), [ocaml/ocaml#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14620))
- **Continuation objects** now use one field instead of two, reducing allocation overhead for effect handlers; by [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk) ([ocaml/ocaml#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14469))
- **S390x TSan** (Thread Sanitizer) support removed, simplifying the s390x backend; by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) ([ocaml/ocaml#14651](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14651))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week concentrated entirely in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml); [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) had no activity during the period
- 15+ contributors active across stdlib additions, GC optimization, type system improvements, and build/cross-platform work
- Key merges include the new `Atomic.Array` module ([ocaml/ocaml#14380](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14380)), `List.append_map`/`rev_append_map` ([ocaml/ocaml#13554](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13554)), and a type soundness fix that previously could cause segfaults ([ocaml/ocaml#14652](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14652))
- A `Bigarray.reshape` overflow vulnerability affecting OCaml 4.14.x was reported ([ocaml/ocaml#14655](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14655)); confirmed fixed as a side effect in OCaml 5, with a security advisory suggested for the 4.14 branch
- Multiple GC optimization efforts active simultaneously: ephemeron quadratic blowup fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14657](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14657)), minor GC hot-path rewrite ([ocaml/ocaml#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571)), ephemeron budget fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14610](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14610)), and minor heap size defaults discussion ([ocaml/ocaml#14634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14634))
- Type error message quality and typing refactoring are active threads with multiple open PRs from [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) and [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob)
group_summaries.activity_summary: New PRs opened for semantic type error highlighting, ephemeron GC improvements, and prefetch enhancements; ongoing work on minor GC, CFI support, and typing.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened a **semantic highlighting PR for type error messages** ([ocaml/ocaml#14659](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14659)) that highlights incompatible subexpressions using color markers, built on a typechecker error trace refactoring ([ocaml/ocaml#14536](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14536))
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) opened an **ephemeron quadratic GC blowup fix** ([ocaml/ocaml#14657](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14657)) that eagerly marks data of preserved ephemerons, reducing collection time from ~0.5s per cycle to effectively instantaneous on deep dependency chains
- [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) opened **`-Wwrite-strings` C compiler flag** enablement ([ocaml/ocaml#14666](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14666)) and **always emit `prefetchw` on x86/x86_64** ([ocaml/ocaml#14665](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14665)) PRs
- **Faster minor GC** rewrite of `oldify_one` hot path by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) remains under review, targeting 2–3% total runtime improvements in promotion-heavy workloads ([ocaml/ocaml#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571))
