- **CFI/DWARF debug info** for Power ([ocaml/ocaml#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482)) and s390x ([ocaml/ocaml#14500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14500)) by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) continues after s390x TSan removal
- **Native `Iatomic_fetch_add`** code generation extended to amd64, arm64, riscv, and power backends by [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) ([ocaml/ocaml#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575)), with benchmarks showing contended multi-domain performance improvements
- **Hashtbl default randomization** discussion ([ocaml/ocaml#14291](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14291)) remains active with consensus forming around `Hashtbl.create_seeded` and `Hashtbl.create_deterministic` rather than changing the default
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) has a PR open for **`{Sys,Unix}.rename` on Windows** for better POSIX compatibility ([ocaml/ocaml#14602](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14602))
- [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) continues a **typing refactoring series** splitting `type_let` into `type_let_nonrec` and `type_let_rec` ([ocaml/ocaml#14394](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14394), [ocaml/ocaml#14537](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14537), [ocaml/ocaml#14538](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14538)) as prerequisite work for fixing polymorphic recursion issues
- [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) has a PR open to **run expect tests with `-rectypes`** flag ([ocaml/ocaml#14643](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14643)) to catch behavioral differences, showing ~10% testsuite time increase
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) also has a PR for **modular explicit optional argument elision** in the Lambda IR ([ocaml/ocaml#14648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14648))
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) opened a **correct compiler flag ordering fix** for ocamltest ([ocaml/ocaml#14662](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14662)) and merged a 4.14 maintenance fix for pthread library linking ([ocaml/ocaml#14661](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14661))
- Stale issues closed during triage: [ocaml/ocaml#12425](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12425), [ocaml/ocaml#12367](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12367), [ocaml/ocaml#12218](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12218), [ocaml/ocaml#11948](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11948)
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Hashtbl default randomization** ([ocaml/ocaml#14291](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14291)): [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) proposed tracking insertion order to make iteration deterministic regardless of randomization; [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) and others raised concern about breakage comparable to `-safe-string`; consensus forming around `Hashtbl.create_seeded` and `Hashtbl.create_deterministic`
- **Minor heap size defaults** ([ocaml/ocaml#14634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14634)): [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) presents benchmark data showing the current 256Kwords (2MiB) default is too conservative — `OCAMLRUNPARAM=s=32M` shows up to 3x speedup on allocation-heavy workloads; discussion ongoing on choosing a better default
- **Polymorphic parameters soundness** ([ocaml/ocaml#14434](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14434)): minimal fix by Stephen Dolan and [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) chosen over a larger refactoring proposed by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob); [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) noted the two-timescale consideration between bug-fix speed and long-term maintainability
- **Mutually-recursive function performance regression** in Coccinelle reported by [Victor Gambier](https://github.com/vgambier) ([ocaml/ocaml#14656](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14656)): chains of 62+ recursive functions cross the 256-word minor-heap allocation boundary, causing closures to be allocated on the major heap; root cause identified by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls)
- **Undesirable exponential typechecking time** for programs with deeply-nested tuple types reported by [Arthur Charguéraud](https://github.com/charguer) ([ocaml/ocaml#14650](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14650)); [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) and [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) discussed whether the behavior is inherent to deep copying and ambivalence/principality tracking
- **`Bigarray.reshape` overflow** reported by [Andriy Sultanov](https://github.com/last-genius) ([ocaml/ocaml#14655](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14655)): allows reading uninitialized memory on OCaml 4.14.x; confirmed fixed in OCaml 5 as a side effect of [ocaml/ocaml#11022](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11022); [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) flagged this as deserving a security advisory for the 4.14 branch
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Atomic/parallel primitives** continue expanding: `Atomic.Array` merged ([ocaml/ocaml#14380](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14380)), native `Iatomic_fetch_add` across all backends progressing ([ocaml/ocaml#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575)), and continuation object size reduction ([ocaml/ocaml#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14469)) indicate sustained investment in multicore performance primitives
- **GC optimization** active on multiple fronts simultaneously: ephemeron quadratic blowup fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14657](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14657)), minor GC rewrite ([ocaml/ocaml#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571)), ephemeron budget fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14610](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14610)), and minor heap size discussion ([ocaml/ocaml#14634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14634))
- **Type error message quality** improving with [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)'s semantic highlighting PR ([ocaml/ocaml#14659](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14659)) built on typechecker error trace refactoring ([ocaml/ocaml#14536](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14536)), continuing a multi-release effort
- **Cross-platform and build system** work by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) spans multiple PRs: MSVC prefetch docs ([ocaml/ocaml#14616](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14616)), lexer fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14654](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14654)), `-Wwrite-strings` ([ocaml/ocaml#14666](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14666)), `prefetchw` emission ([ocaml/ocaml#14665](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14665)), and `LN_S`/`AR` standardization ([ocaml/ocaml#14552](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14552))
- **Typing refactoring towards `let rec` improvements**: [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob)'s multi-PR series ([ocaml/ocaml#14394](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14394), [ocaml/ocaml#14537](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14537), [ocaml/ocaml#14538](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14538)) separating recursive and non-recursive `let` typing is groundwork for fixing long-standing polymorphic recursion issues ([ocaml/ocaml#14253](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14253))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Picos merged an OCaml 5.5 compatibility fix; Eio received two new issues around API usability in Path and Flow.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: OCaml 5.5 compatibility fix merged in picos_std.awaitable (picos#387).
group_summaries.new_features: - **OCaml 5.5 compatibility** fix in `picos_std.awaitable` by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate): adds an explicit type constraint `(One tail_r : [ \`One ] tdt)` in the `snoc` function, addressing a type-checking change in OCaml 5.5 (see [ocaml/ocaml#14224](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14224)) ([ocaml-multicore/picos#387](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/387))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was limited to two repositories: [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) and [ocaml-multicore/picos](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos); the remaining seven repositories had no recorded activity.
- [ocaml-multicore/picos](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos) merged one PR — a one-line OCaml 5.5 compatibility fix ([ocaml-multicore/picos#387](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/387)) — with discussion around cutting a release for ecosystem beta-testing.
- [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) received two new issues from [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) covering a race condition in `Eio.Path.load` ([ocaml-multicore/eio#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835)) and a proposal to add `Close` support to `Eio.Flow.buffer_sink` ([ocaml-multicore/eio#834](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/834)), plus renewed student engagement on the long-standing lock-free `Waiters` issue ([ocaml-multicore/eio#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/382)).
group_summaries.activity_summary: Eio received two new usability issues around Path.load and buffer_sink; picos discussion focused on timing a new release for OCaml 5.5.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) opened [ocaml-multicore/eio#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) reporting that `Eio.Path.load` has a stat-then-read race condition and silently returns empty strings for `/proc` virtual files (where `stat` reports size 0); the proposed fix is to treat file size only as a hint for initial buffer allocation.
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) opened [ocaml-multicore/eio#834](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/834) proposing that `Eio.Flow.buffer_sink` be made closable, with open design questions about semantics (no-op vs. error on subsequent writes) and backwards-compatibility.
- [Mitesh Sharma](https://github.com/Miteshryp) commented on the long-standing [ocaml-multicore/eio#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/382) (lock-free `Waiters`, originally opened by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) in 2022), asking whether a CQS-based implementation from a supervised course project has been completed.
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) followed up with [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) on the possibility of a picos release to support OCaml 5.5 beta-testers and the wider ecosystem ([ocaml-multicore/picos#387](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/387)).
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- The picos OCaml 5.5 fix ([ocaml-multicore/picos#387](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/387)) directly tracks a type-checking change introduced upstream in [ocaml/ocaml#14224](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14224), illustrating the dependency of multicore ecosystem libraries on compiler evolution.
- The release discussion in [ocaml-multicore/picos#387](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/387) signals broader ecosystem readiness work: a picos release would unblock OCaml 5.5 beta-testing for downstream users of picos-based concurrency abstractions.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [ocaml-multicore/eio#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835): [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) includes a concrete reproducer showing `"/proc/sys/fs/file-nr"` returning `""` via `Eio.Path.load` while `cat` returns the expected content, highlighting a silent data-loss scenario for `/proc` virtual files.
- [ocaml-multicore/eio#834](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/834): proposal to extend `Eio.Flow.buffer_sink` with a `Close` capability raises open API design questions about backwards-compatibility and close semantics for in-memory buffer sinks.
- [ocaml-multicore/eio#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/382): student engagement from [Mitesh Sharma](https://github.com/Miteshryp) on a coursework CQS implementation (supervised by `@kayceesrk`) points to ongoing academic use of Eio's synchronization internals as a research and teaching vehicle.
- [ocaml-multicore/picos#387](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/387): [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) raised the question of releasing picos with the OCaml 5.5 fix to [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic), surfacing the release-coordination challenge for multicore libraries ahead of a compiler beta.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- Two issues filed by the same external contributor ([Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog)) in a single week — [ocaml-multicore/eio#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835) and [ocaml-multicore/eio#834](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/834) — indicate growing external adoption uncovering edge cases in `Eio.Path` and `Eio.Flow`, particularly around `/proc` file handling and composability with close-aware consumers.
- Academic use of multicore libraries continues: student coursework on CQS-based lock-free synchronization referencing [ocaml-multicore/eio#382](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/382) suggests Eio's internals are being used as a teaching and research platform.
- OCaml 5.5 readiness is becoming a coordination point: the picos fix ([ocaml-multicore/picos#387](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/387)) and associated release discussion reflect a pattern of multicore libraries tracking upstream compiler changes to support beta-testing pipelines.

weekly_summary.brief_summary: Dune 3.22.0 stable released; OCaml 4.14.3 LTS security patch (CVE-2026-28364); compiler gains Atomic.Array and List combinators.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: Dune 3.22.0 @revdep aliases; ocaml-lsp 1.26.0-5.5~preview; Atomic.Array module; opam PowerShell completions; js_of_ocaml 28-91% V8 register reduction.
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [Dune 3.22.0](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) shipped **@revdep, @revdep-check, and @revdep-runtest aliases** ([ocaml/dune#13800](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13800)) for building reverse dependencies, plus **`dune promote` path-prefix and `--file` filtering** ([ocaml/dune#13876](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13876)) and **custom action trace events** with a new `dune-action-trace` library.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **ocaml-lsp 1.26.0-5.5~preview** released with a new **destruct custom LSP request** ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583)), **environment-variable build-system configuration** (`$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM`, `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_ROOT`), and **improved signature-help readability** ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1580)).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **opam gained PowerShell tab-completions** via a cmdliner 2.1.0 update ([ocaml/opam#6869](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6869) by [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian)), replacing the previous bash/zsh-only approach and closing [ocaml/opam#6853](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6853) and the long-standing [ocaml/opam#684](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/684).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **js_of_ocaml variable coalescing** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166)) reduces V8 register usage by **28–91%**, and a **Wasm bigstring DataView optimisation** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144)) improves non-V8 engine performance.
- __RUMINANT:core__ A new **`Atomic.Array` module** ([ocaml/ocaml#14380](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14380) by [@clef-men](https://github.com/clef-men) and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)) adds safe and unsafe atomic array operations (`make`, `init`, `get`/`set`, `exchange`, `compare_and_set`, `fetch_and_add`).
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`List.append_map` and `List.rev_append_map`** ([ocaml/ocaml#13554](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13554) by [Sylvain Boilard](https://github.com/SylvainBoilard) and Hugo Heuzard) provide tail-recursive-modulo-cons list combinators.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Set.S.singleton_to_elt` and `Map.S.singleton_to_binding`** ([ocaml/ocaml#14121](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14121) by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls)) add singleton extraction utilities to the Set and Map standard library modules.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Hashtbl.is_randomized`** exposed in `Sys.runtime_parameters` ([ocaml/ocaml#14292](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14292) by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm), [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27), [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA)) allows runtime introspection of hash-table randomisation state.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Bytecode optimization hints** ([ocaml/ocaml#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13438) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)) are now preserved in bytecode output for Js_of_ocaml and Wasm_of_ocaml consumption.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Prefetch read/write distinction** ([ocaml/ocaml#14570](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14570), [ocaml/ocaml#14616](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14616) by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) and [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA)) differentiates prefetch operations for improved code generation.
- __RUMINANT:core__ A **type soundness fix** for polymorphic parameter checking ([ocaml/ocaml#14434](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14434), [ocaml/ocaml#14652](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14652) by Stephen Dolan and [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue)) and a **bytecode effects heap-corruption fix** in `reperform` ([ocaml/ocaml#14644](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14644), [ocaml/ocaml#14647](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14647) by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls)) address correctness regressions.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Continuation objects reduced from two fields to one** ([ocaml/ocaml#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14469) by [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk)) lowers allocation overhead for effect-based code.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **UTF-8 string processing optimisations** ([ocaml/ocaml#14618](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14618), [ocaml/ocaml#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14620) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel)) and a **`Float.Array.sub`/`append`/`concat` bug fix** ([ocaml/ocaml#14635](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14635)) round out stdlib improvements.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **eval beta-reduction in type expressions** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5323](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5323), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5402](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5402) by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski)) introduces a `Tquote_eval` type constructor with `reduce_head` for staged computation in types.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Mode constraint syntax without a type annotation** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5602](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5602) by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke)) makes `(x @ local)` valid syntax, improving ergonomics of mode annotations.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **CamlinternalQuote API expansion** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5649](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5649), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5650](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5650), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5645](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5645) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)) adds `Identifier.eval`, `Vb_attribute` module, and `let_rec_simple` updates.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ The **x86 binary emitter** gained missing instruction encodings and an ASAN failure fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5653](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5653) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)), and **Camlinternaleval is now compiled at `-O3`** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5646](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5646)).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ Two **Reaper bug fixes** were merged: incorrect argument count ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5633](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5633) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)) and a crash when loading mutable variables ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5655](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5655) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Merlin upstream sync** incorporating 5.2.0minus-34 changes with layout polymorphism support ([oxcaml/merlin#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)).
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **picos OCaml 5.5 compatibility fix** in `picos_std.awaitable` ([ocaml-multicore/picos#387](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/387) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)) adds an explicit type constraint to the `snoc` function, tracking upstream [ocaml/ocaml#14224](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14224).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **OCaml 4.14.3 LTS** released with a security fix for CVE-2026-28364 (Marshal deserialization buffer over-read, OSEC-2026-01), with announcement at [ocaml/ocaml.org#3571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3571).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **ocamlformat 0.29.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29553](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29553)) adds OCaml 5.5 syntax support, an Odoc 3.0 parser, and a `letop-punning` formatting option.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **atd 4.0.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29552](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29552), 11 packages) adds experimental module support and an `atdml` code generator.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ New libraries published: **parseff 0.1.0/0.2.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29526](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29526), [ocaml/opam-repository#29573](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29573)) — direct-style effect-based parser combinators with zero-copy spans; **raven 1.0.0~alpha3** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29490](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29490), 11 packages) — scientific computing suite including `nx-oxcaml` (pure-OCaml SIMD) and `kaun-board` TUI; **jws 0.0.1** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29557](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29557)) — RFC 7515 JWS implementation; **dune-ai-context 0.1.2** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29536](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29536)) — dune external dependency context for AI tools.

-**UTF-8 string processing optimisations** ([ocaml/ocaml#14618](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14618), [ocaml/ocaml#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14620) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [@smuenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel)) and a **`Float.Array.sub`/`append`/`concat` bug fix** ([ocaml/ocaml#14635](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14635)) round out stdlib improvements.
tools: -[Dune 3.22.0](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) shipped **@revdep, @revdep-check, and @revdep-runtest aliases** ([ocaml/dune#13800](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13800)) for building reverse dependencies, plus **`dune promote` path-prefix and `--file` filtering** ([ocaml/dune#13876](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13876)) and **custom action trace events** with a new `dune-action-trace` library.

-**ocaml-lsp 1.26.0-5.5~preview** released with a new **destruct custom LSP request** ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583)), **environment-variable build-system configuration** (`$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM`, `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_ROOT`), and **improved signature-help readability** ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1580)).

-**opam gained PowerShell tab-completions** via a cmdliner 2.1.0 update ([ocaml/opam#6869](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6869) by [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian)), replacing the previous bash/zsh-only approach and closing [ocaml/opam#6853](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6853) and the long-standing [ocaml/opam#684](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/684).

-**js_of_ocaml variable coalescing** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166)) reduces V8 register usage by **28–91%**, and a **Wasm bigstring DataView optimisation** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144)) improves non-V8 engine performance.
ecosystem: -**OCaml 4.14.3 LTS** released with a security fix for CVE-2026-28364 (Marshal deserialization buffer over-read, OSEC-2026-01), with announcement at [ocaml/ocaml.org#3571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3571).

-**ocamlformat 0.29.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29553](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29553)) adds OCaml 5.5 syntax support, an Odoc 3.0 parser, and a `letop-punning` formatting option.
-**atd 4.0.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29552](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29552), 11 packages) adds experimental module support and an `atdml` code generator.

-New libraries published: **parseff 0.1.0/0.2.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29526](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29526), [ocaml/opam-repository#29573](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29573)) — direct-style effect-based parser combinators with zero-copy spans; **raven 1.0.0~alpha3** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29490](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29490), 11 packages) — scientific computing suite including `nx-oxcaml` (pure-OCaml SIMD) and `kaun-board` TUI; **jws 0.0.1** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29557](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29557)) — RFC 7515 JWS implementation; **dune-ai-context 0.1.2** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29536](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29536)) — dune external dependency context for AI tools.
oxcaml: -**eval beta-reduction in type expressions** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5323](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5323), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5402](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5402) by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski)) introduces a `Tquote_eval` type constructor with `reduce_head` for staged computation in types.

-**Mode constraint syntax without a type annotation** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5602](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5602) by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke)) makes `(x @ local)` valid syntax, improving ergonomics of mode annotations.

-**CamlinternalQuote API expansion** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5649](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5649), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5650](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5650), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5645](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5645) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)) adds `Identifier.eval`, `Vb_attribute` module, and `let_rec_simple` updates.

-The **x86 binary emitter** gained missing instruction encodings and an ASAN failure fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5653](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5653) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)), and **Camlinternaleval is now compiled at `-O3`** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5646](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5646)).

-Two **Reaper bug fixes** were merged: incorrect argument count ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5633](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5633) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)) and a crash when loading mutable variables ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5655](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5655) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)).

-**Merlin upstream sync** incorporating 5.2.0minus-34 changes with layout polymorphism support ([oxcaml/merlin#225](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/225) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)).
multicore: -**picos OCaml 5.5 compatibility fix** in `picos_std.awaitable` ([ocaml-multicore/picos#387](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/387) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)) adds an explicit type constraint to the `snoc` function, tracking upstream [ocaml/ocaml#14224](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14224).

weekly_summary.group_overview:
- __RUMINANT:core__ The compiler merged 15+ PRs this week, with the most user-visible additions being the new **`Atomic.Array` module** ([ocaml/ocaml#14380](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14380)), **`List.append_map`/`List.rev_append_map`** ([ocaml/ocaml#13554](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13554)), and **Set/Map singleton utilities** ([ocaml/ocaml#14121](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14121)), alongside correctness fixes for bytecode effects ([ocaml/ocaml#14644](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14644)) and type soundness ([ocaml/ocaml#14434](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14434)).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ OxCaml saw 34 new PRs opened and 13+ merged, with the **mode system** advancing through [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke)'s annotation-syntax work ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5602](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5602)), [Jules Jacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) opening 4 **mode-bitfields** PRs, and [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) progressing the **staging/quoting** subsystem with eval beta-reduction ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5323](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5323)).
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ Activity was light: one compatibility fix merged for **picos OCaml 5.5** ([ocaml-multicore/picos#387](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/387)), two new Eio issues opened covering a **race condition in `Eio.Path.load`** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#835](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/835)) and a proposal for **`Close` support in `Eio.Flow.buffer_sink`** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#834](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/834)).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) published **Dune 3.22.0** as a stable release ([ocaml/dune#13769](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13769)) with 13,512 downloads in the first day; [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) released **ocaml-lsp 1.26.0-5.5~preview** with a destruct custom request; opam gained **PowerShell completions** via cmdliner 2.1.0; and js_of_ocaml landed major **variable coalescing** work reducing V8 register pressure by 28–91%.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ The **OCaml 4.14.3 LTS** security release (CVE-2026-28364) and **Dune 3.22.0** entry into opam-repository were the dominant events, alongside **ocamlformat 0.29.0** with OCaml 5.5 support, **atd 4.0.0**, and several new library submissions including `parseff` and `raven`.

weekly_summary.activity_summary: Compiler has 15+ open PRs including minor GC rewrite, DWARF debug-info, and Windows rename; OxCaml progresses upstream 5.4 merge and x86 push/pop spill replacement.
weekly_summary.activity:
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) has a **faster minor GC** PR ([ocaml/ocaml#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571)) open that rewrites `oldify_one` for improved throughput, building on discussions about minor heap size defaults that show a **3× speedup at `s=32M`** ([ocaml/ocaml#14634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14634)).
- __RUMINANT:core__ CFI/DWARF debug-info improvements for **Power and s390x** ([ocaml/ocaml#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14482), [ocaml/ocaml#14500](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14500)) and **native `Iatomic_fetch_add` on amd64, arm64, riscv, power** ([ocaml/ocaml#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575)) are under review.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Semantic highlighting for type errors** ([ocaml/ocaml#14659](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14659) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)) and a **modular explicit optional argument elision** PR ([ocaml/ocaml#14648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14648)) are awaiting review.
- __RUMINANT:core__ A **Windows `{Sys,Unix}.rename`** fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14602](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14602) by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb)) and an **ephemeron quadratic GC blow-up fix** ([ocaml/ocaml#14657](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14657) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole)) are in open review.
- __RUMINANT:core__ Typing refactoring continues with [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob)'s `type_let_nonrec`/`type_let_rec` split ([ocaml/ocaml#14394](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14394), [ocaml/ocaml#14537](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14537), [ocaml/ocaml#14538](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14538)) progressing through review.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ The **5.4 upstream merge** reached successful compile/link ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5598](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5598) by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes)) representing a major milestone in keeping OxCaml current with upstream OCaml.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) opened three register-allocator improvement PRs: **leaf-function clobber tracking** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5656](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5656)), **architecture-dependent spilling costs** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5659](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5659)), and a `Printcfg` module replacing ad-hoc CFG dumps ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5632](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5632)).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) opened x86 backend optimisation work: **push/pop spill replacement** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5635](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5635)), **x86 peephole optimisations** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5639](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5639)), and an **optimisation-fuel infrastructure with Python bisect** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5651](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5651)).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) opened `Gc.Memprof.participate_globally` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5644](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5644)) and `Atomic.make_contended` runtime4 compatibility ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5640](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5640)), while [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) submitted **domain liveness tracking** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5638](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5638)).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ The **ARM64 branch relaxation** effort ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5499](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5499)) addressed 5 subtle issues identified by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) and reviewed by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ Dune cache consolidation merged two refactors: `dune_cache_storage` into `dune_cache` ([ocaml/dune#13840](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13840)) and `dune_rpc_private` into `dune_rpc` ([ocaml/dune#13854](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13854)), plus safer `Stdlib.Marshal` wrapping ([ocaml/dune#13883](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13883)) and a value-sharing digest bug fix ([ocaml/dune#13884](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13884)).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ Multiple **Windows fixes** landed in Dune: binary lookup stripping `.exe` from keys ([ocaml/dune#13856](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13856)), `open_out_bin` to avoid CRLF ([ocaml/dune#13838](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13838)), and `.exe` handling in `.install` files ([ocaml/dune#13864](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13864)).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ js_of_ocaml is tracking **OCaml 5.5 adaptation** in a 12,000-line draft ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195)), while **Wasm Stack Switching for native effects** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189)) and **Wasm dynlink/toplevel unification** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)) are under review.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **odoc OxCaml upstream integration** ([ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399) by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w)) and a **polymorphic parameter parentheses fix** ([ocaml/odoc#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404)) are open, with OCaml 5.5 tracking at [ocaml/odoc#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401).

-Pending opam-repository submissions include **ppxlib 0.38.0** with OCaml 5.5 and bivariant type parameters ([ocaml/opam-repository#29563](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29563)), **Ortac 0.8.0** with QCheck-STM+Domains parallel testing ([ocaml/opam-repository#29564](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29564)), **lsp 1.26.0-5.5~preview** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29565](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29565)), and **alcobar 0.3** for Alcotest-compatible fuzzing ([ocaml/opam-repository#29556](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29556)).
oxcaml: -The **5.4 upstream merge** reached successful compile/link ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5598](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5598) by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes)) representing a major milestone in keeping OxCaml current with upstream OCaml.

-[Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) opened three register-allocator improvement PRs: **leaf-function clobber tracking** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5656](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5656)), **architecture-dependent spilling costs** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5659](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5659)), and a `Printcfg` module replacing ad-hoc CFG dumps ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5632](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5632)).

-[@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) opened x86 backend optimisation work: **push/pop spill replacement** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5635](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5635)), **x86 peephole optimisations** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5639](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5639)), and an **optimisation-fuel infrastructure with Python bisect** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5651](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5651)).

-[Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) opened `Gc.Memprof.participate_globally` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5644](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5644)) and `Atomic.make_contended` runtime4 compatibility ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5640](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5640)), while [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) submitted **domain liveness tracking** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5638](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5638)).

-The **ARM64 branch relaxation** effort ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5499](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5499)) addressed 5 subtle issues identified by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) and reviewed by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell).

weekly_summary.cross_repository_work:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Dune 3.22.0** required coordinated constraint updates across [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) for stdune ([ocaml/opam-repository#29561](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29561)), dune-rpc ([ocaml/opam-repository#29555](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29555)), and ocamlformat ([ocaml/opam-repository#29568](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29568)); [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) also filed [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1594) to update ocaml-lsp compilation against Dune 3.22.
- __RUMINANT:core__ __RUMINANT:tools__ **Bytecode optimization hints** ([ocaml/ocaml#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13438)) merged in the compiler directly enables js_of_ocaml and Wasm_of_ocaml to consume these hints; this completes a chain of work tracked since week 11.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ __RUMINANT:tools__ **OxCaml `.cms` file support** in Dune 3.22.0 and **odoc OxCaml upstream integration** ([ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399)) form part of the ongoing effort to make OxCaml's extended features accessible through standard tooling; js_of_ocaml is also tracking OxCaml support at [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105).
- __RUMINANT:core__ __RUMINANT:multicore__ The **picos OCaml 5.5 compatibility fix** ([ocaml-multicore/picos#387](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/387)) was driven by the upstream compiler change [ocaml/ocaml#14224](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14224), illustrating the rolling compatibility work required as OCaml 5.5 approaches release.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OCaml 5.5 compatibility tracking** spans multiple tool repositories this week: ocaml-lsp at [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1596](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1596), odoc at [ocaml/odoc#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401), ocamlfind at [ocaml/ocamlfind#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115), and js_of_ocaml at [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195) — each tracking different aspects of the parsetree and runtime changes.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Windows platform work** spanned both opam and dune: opam received MSYS2 `GetCurrentProcessToken` via `_WIN32_WINNT` Windows 8.1 ([ocaml/opam#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862)), dune fixed `.exe` handling ([ocaml/dune#13856](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13856), [ocaml/dune#13864](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13864)), and the opam-repository received 4 new MSYS2 depext issues plus `ocaml-option-clang-cl` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29582](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29582)).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **MirageOS conditional lwt constraints** discussion ([ocaml/opam-repository#29569](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29569) with [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm), [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate), [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri)) concerns how to manage the lwt 5/6 split across Mirage packages in opam-repository, connecting to the larger lwt 6 migration affecting 84 packages.

weekly_summary.notable_discussions:
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Hashtbl default randomisation** ([ocaml/ocaml#14291](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14291)) generated ongoing debate between [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) about whether making randomisation the default would break deterministic programs or improve security posture across the ecosystem.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Minor heap size defaults** discussion ([ocaml/ocaml#14634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14634)) centred on [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)'s benchmarks showing a **3× speedup with `s=32M`** compared to the current default, raising the question of whether the GC tuning defaults should be revisited for modern hardware.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Bigarray.reshape overflow vulnerability** ([ocaml/ocaml#14655](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14655) by [Andriy Sultanov](https://github.com/last-genius)) raised a security advisory discussion with [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) about the buffer over-read risk, connecting to the CVE-2026-28364 fix in OCaml 4.14.3.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Exponential typechecking for deeply-nested tuples** ([ocaml/ocaml#14650](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14650)) was reported by [Arthur Charguéraud](https://github.com/charguer) with [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) and [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob) investigating the algorithmic complexity issue.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Mutually-recursive function performance regression** ([ocaml/ocaml#14656](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14656) by [Victor Gambier](https://github.com/vgambier)) was analysed by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) who identified it as a code-generation issue rather than a typechecking problem.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ The **write/observable mode diamond** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608)) design discussion between [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke), [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere), and [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) concerns undocumented mode-implication relationships and whether the diamond structure is the right abstraction.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Poisoned/poisoning modes** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5666](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5666) by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke)) prompted a safety discussion with [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) and [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) about the semantics of propagating poison through the mode lattice.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune CI job timing** debate ([ocaml/dune#13844](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13844)) weighed 20–30 minute per-package dependency check runs on every PR versus only on main merges, with the current approach chosen to catch breakage earlier.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune Windows 7 bootstrap** ([ocaml/dune#13871](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13871)) revealed that `GetSystemTimePreciseAsFileTime` is a Windows 8 function; [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) is preparing a fallback for older Windows versions.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Merlin `locate` 'already at origin' bug** ([ocaml/merlin#2033](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2033)) was analysed by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) with a deep dive into `locate.ml` — ultimately closing [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1586](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1586) in favour of the Merlin fix.

weekly_summary.emerging_trends:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **OCaml 5.5 compatibility preparation** is now active across every major tool simultaneously — ocaml-lsp ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1596](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1596)), odoc ([ocaml/odoc#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401)), ocamlfind ([ocaml/ocamlfind#115](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/115)), js_of_ocaml ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195)), picos ([ocaml-multicore/picos#387](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/387)), and the opam ecosystem — with the picos fix and 12,000-line js_of_ocaml draft representing the scale of adaptation needed.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ The **OxCaml mode system** is expanding rapidly: [Jules Jacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs)' mode-bitfields series ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5671](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5671)–[oxcaml/oxcaml#5677](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5677)), [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke)'s poisoned/poisoning modes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5666](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5666)), and the write/observable diamond discussion ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5608](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5608)) indicate a period of rapid mode-system exploration, with the `@local` annotation syntax now more ergonomic.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Unboxed representations and user-defined layouts** are a growing focus with [Jules Jacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) opening constructor-level `[@repr ...]` annotations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5678](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5678)), user-defined `[@@or_null]` types ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5668](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5668)), recursive unboxed product dummy layout ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5667](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5667)), and local abstract kind substitutions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5665](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5665)).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Windows and cross-platform coverage** is receiving sustained investment across dune, opam, and the opam-repository simultaneously, with [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto)'s MSYS2 depext sweep, the `ocaml-option-clang-cl` package ([ocaml/opam-repository#29582](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29582)), Merlin GNU Hurd `PATH_MAX` portability ([ocaml/merlin#2039](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2039)), and dune's Windows bootstrap and `.exe` fixes.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **WebAssembly as a first-class target** continues to grow in scope: js_of_ocaml is developing **native effects via Stack Switching** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189)), **dynlink/toplevel unification** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)), and Wasm bigstring DataView optimisations ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2144](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2144)).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Effect-based programming** is reaching library maturity: `parseff` introduces direct-style parser combinators using algebraic effects ([ocaml/opam-repository#29526](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29526)), while the compiler's continuation-object size reduction ([ocaml/ocaml#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14469)) and bytecode hints preservation ([ocaml/ocaml#13438](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13438)) lower the runtime overhead of effect-based code.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Scientific computing** is establishing an OCaml presence with the `raven` 1.0.0~alpha3 suite ([ocaml/opam-repository#29490](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29490)) including `nx-oxcaml` (pure-OCaml SIMD leveraging OxCaml's unboxed types) — a pattern of OxCaml-accelerated numerical code appearing in ecosystem packages.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **GC tuning and performance** is attracting attention: the minor heap size defaults discussion ([ocaml/ocaml#14634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14634)), Nick Barnes' `oldify_one` rewrite ([ocaml/ocaml#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571)), and the `Atomic.Array` addition ([ocaml/ocaml#14380](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14380)) together point toward ongoing work to improve OCaml's performance ceiling for concurrent and memory-intensive workloads.

-**Unboxed representations and user-defined layouts** are a growing focus with [Jules Jacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) opening constructor-level `[@repr ...]` annotations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5678](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5678)), user-defined `[@@or_null]` types ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5668](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5668)), recursive unboxed product dummy layout ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5667](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5667)), and local abstract kind substitutions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5665](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5665)).
